/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:8766:## Reflection
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:8770:## Recommended Next Focus
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:8783:# Iteration 009: COMPARISON - LIFECYCLE
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:8785:## Focus
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:8788:## Findings
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:8789:### Finding 1: Modus boots a vault process, not a resumable session lifecycle
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:8791:- **What it does**: Modus resolves one vault root from flag/env/default, creates core directories, builds one in-memory index, registers 11 tools, and starts a stdio MCP loop. Its MCP initialize path only announces the tool surface, and its only built-in lifecycle/status tool is `vault_status`, which returns vault counts rather than any session/task recovery contract.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:8792:- **Why it matters for us**: Modus is process-and-vault scoped; Public is session-and-task scoped. Modus has no real analogue for `memory_context`, `session_resume`, or `session_bootstrap`.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:8793:- **Recommendation**: reject
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:8794:- **Impact**: high
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:8796:### Finding 2: Modus’s only session field is decorative, not an enforced partition
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:8798:- **What it does**: Indexed facts may carry `session_tag`, defaulting to `session-1` when absent, but the fact store indexes only subject/predicate/value and scores only term overlap, confidence, and recency. `memory_store` does not write `session_tag` at all.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:8799:- **Why it matters for us**: There is no true session boundary in retrieval or storage. Public already does far more by threading `sessionId` through retrieval, dedup, and continuity logic.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:8800:- **Recommendation**: reject
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:8801:- **Impact**: high
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:8803:### Finding 3: Public’s trusted session lifecycle is a real control plane; Modus has no equivalent
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:8805:- **What it does**: Public resolves only server-managed session IDs, rejects mismatched callers, persists `session_state` with `spec_folder` and `current_task`, tracks inferred mode, and can inject attended working-memory items into resumed prompt context. Modus exposes no session token, no identity binding, and no resumed prompt-context surface.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:8806:- **Why it matters for us**: Public’s continuity model is explicit and guarded; Modus’s continuity is implicit in whatever markdown already exists in the vault.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:8807:- **Recommendation**: reject
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:8808:- **Impact**: high
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:8810:### Finding 4: Public’s resume/bootstrap path is compositional and fail-closed; Modus only offers raw vault health
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:8812:- **What it does**: `session_resume` composes `memory_context(resume)`, code-graph status, CocoIndex availability, structural context, and cached continuity only when summary text, transcript identity, freshness, and scope all validate. `session_bootstrap` adds health, hints, and next actions. Modus has no comparable resume packet or cached-summary validator; `vault_status` is just counts.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:8813:- **Why it matters for us**: Public is already materially ahead on recovery lifecycle. Modus does not contribute a reusable bootstrap pattern here.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:8814:- **Recommendation**: reject
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:8815:- **Impact**: high
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:8817:### Finding 5: Modus’s write lifecycle is immediate markdown mutation, but it is not same-session freshness-safe
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:8819:- **What it does**: In Modus, `vault_write` and `memory_store` write markdown directly, but the searchable index is built at startup and queried in memory with no post-write refresh in the write path. Public’s `generate-context` flow is structured-input, path-validated, atomically written, post-reviewed, and immediately indexed.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:8820:- **Why it matters for us**: This is a lifecycle correctness gap, not just a style difference. Modus is simpler, but same-process writes can lag behind search until rebuild/restart. Public’s heavier save pipeline is justified for authoritative memory artifacts.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:8821:- **Recommendation**: reject
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:8822:- **Impact**: high
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:8824:### Finding 6: Modus treats recall as an implicit global write; Public’s two-timescale lifecycle is safer
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:8826:- **What it does**: Modus `memory_search` asynchronously reinforces every returned fact, mutating confidence, stability, difficulty, `last_accessed`, and `access_count` on read. Public intentionally separates ephemeral session attention from long-term memory decay: resume retrieval disables long-term decay, while working-memory attention drives bounded prompt-context injection.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:8827:- **Why it matters for us**: The transferable idea is that recall events matter, but Modus implements that as implicit global mutation. For Public, any equivalent should be explicit and scoped.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:8828:- **Recommendation**: prototype later
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:8829:- **Impact**: medium
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:8831:## Sources Consulted
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:8850:## Assessment
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:8855:## Reflection
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:8859:## Recommended Next Focus
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:8872:# Iteration 010: COMPARISON - HYGIENE
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:8874:## Focus
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:8877:## Findings
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:8878:### Finding 1: Modus’s MCP health surface is thinner than advertised, and thinner than Public’s actual hygiene control plane
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:8880:- **What it does**: Modus exposes `vault_status` as its only MCP-visible hygiene/status tool in the memory toolset, and the tool description claims “file counts, index size, cross-ref stats.” But `StatusJSON()` only returns `total_files`, `index_docs`, and a top-level directory breakdown. The richer cross-ref counts are only computed inside the separate `doctor` CLI. Public’s `memory_health`, by contrast, reports database/connectivity state, embedding/provider metadata, alias conflicts, FTS drift, orphan cleanup outcomes, and gated `autoRepair`.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:8881:- **Why it matters for us**: For agent-facing hygiene, status needs to be both truthful and actionable. Modus splits diagnostics between MCP and an offline CLI and under-delivers on the MCP side; Public already has the stronger operational pattern.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:8882:- **Recommendation**: reject
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:8883:- **Impact**: high
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:8885:### Finding 2: Modus’s `doctor` has one reusable hygiene idea Public does not fully cover yet: content-level contradiction and duplicate-fact linting
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:8887:- **What it does**: Modus `doctor` explicitly scans fact files for missing `subject`/`predicate`, duplicate `subject+predicate` pairs, and “same subject, same predicate, different value” contradictions. Public `memory_health` is stronger on infrastructure integrity and repair, but its current checks center on schema/DB/FTS/alias/vector/orphan health. Public’s contradiction tooling today is causal-edge oriented, not fact-content oriented.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:8888:- **Why it matters for us**: This is the clearest hygiene gap where Modus has a useful idea. A content-level contradiction/duplicate pass would complement Public’s existing infra health tooling instead of duplicating it.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:8889:- **Recommendation**: prototype later
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:8890:- **Impact**: medium
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:8892:### Finding 3: Modus cross-references are associative adjacency maps, not a causal-memory hygiene system
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:8894:- **What it does**: Modus builds an in-memory `bySubject`/`byTag`/`byEntity` cross-index, scores connections with fixed weights (subject `3.0`, entity `2.0`, tag `1.0`), and appends connected docs to search results or returns them via `vault_connected`. Public stores typed causal edges (`caused`, `enabled`, `supersedes`, `contradicts`, `derived_from`, `supports`), exposes traversal in both directions, tracks graph coverage/orphans, records `weight_history`, and applies contradiction detection and edge bounds.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:8895:- **Why it matters for us**: Modus’s cross-links are useful for loose discovery, but they do not preserve decision lineage or graph hygiene. They cannot answer the same class of “why was this decision made?” questions that Public’s causal graph can.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:8896:- **Recommendation**: reject
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:8897:- **Impact**: high
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:8899:### Finding 4: Modus has no trigger-recall plane; its query hygiene stops at query expansion
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:8901:- **What it does**: Modus exposes search plus Librarian expansion, but no trigger-phrase cache, no prompt-signal vocabulary, no scope/session validation, no degraded-mode reporting for trigger sources, and no co-activation or tiered-context recall path. Public’s trigger system caches validated phrases from `memory_index`, uses Unicode-aware boundary matching plus candidate indexing, fails closed on scope errors, reports degraded source parsing, supports signal boosts, and layers session-aware decay/co-activation/tier filtering on top.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:8902:- **Why it matters for us**: Trigger recall is a hygiene/control feature as much as a retrieval feature: it governs safe latent-context surfacing. Modus does not offer a comparable mechanism to borrow here.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:8903:- **Recommendation**: reject
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:8904:- **Impact**: high
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:8906:### Finding 5: Modus’s markdown pipeline is intentionally permissive, but that permissiveness weakens authoritative memory hygiene
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:8908:- **What it does**: Modus treats malformed YAML frontmatter as raw body, skips parse failures during directory scans, writes frontmatter/body directly with no validation gate, and handles duplicate fact writes by suffixing filenames (`-2`, `-3`, ...). Public’s `memory_save` path runs explicit preflight checks for anchors, duplicates, tokens, and size, supports dry-run, and rejects invalid saves unless the caller deliberately bypasses preflight.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:8909:- **Why it matters for us**: Modus is optimized for lightweight human-editable vault ergonomics, not for high-integrity memory ingestion. That tradeoff is fine for a personal markdown vault, but it is weaker than Public’s current hygiene bar for durable indexed memory artifacts.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:8910:- **Recommendation**: reject
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:8911:- **Impact**: high
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:8913:## Sources Consulted
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:8929:## Assessment
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:8934:## Reflection
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:8938:## Recommended Next Focus
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:8951:# Iteration 011: GAP ANALYSIS - MISSING FEATURES
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:8953:## Focus
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:8956:## Findings
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:8958:### Finding 1: Modus has a **prompt-ready operational learnings plane**, not just retrieval feedback
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:8960:- **What it does**: Modus stores cross-model operational lessons in `brain/learnings/*.md` with `domain`, `type`, `severity`, `confidence`, and `reinforced` metadata; `LoadByDomain` always includes `general` and `critical` items, `FormatForPrompt` renders them into `DO / AVOID / DECIDED / CORRECTED` prompt bullets, and MCP exposes list/record/search/reinforce/deprecate operations. Public’s reviewed learning path is `runBatchLearning`, which is explicitly **shadow-only** and does not create a reusable prompt-facing operational memory layer.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:8961:- **Why it matters for us**: This is a different capability from search ranking. It gives the system a place to remember “how to behave” across sessions and agents, not just “what to retrieve.”
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:8962:- **Recommendation**: NEW FEATURE
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:8963:- **Impact**: high
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:8965:### Finding 2: Modus has a **promotion and retirement lifecycle for lessons**, which Public does not visibly expose
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:8967:- **What it does**: Recording an existing learning reinforces it instead of overwriting it; `PromoteFromLesson` escalates an agent-local lesson into a global learning; `RecordCorrection` turns a supervisor correction into a high-confidence shared rule; `Deprecate` sets confidence to `0.0`, and `loadFromDir` then suppresses deprecated learnings from future injection. In the Public files reviewed, learning signals stay observational/shadow-only rather than becoming first-class operational directives with explicit retirement.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:8968:- **Why it matters for us**: Public has feedback and validation signals, but not a clear “candidate lesson -> proven operational rule -> deprecated obsolete rule” pipeline. That would close the loop between reflection, governance, and future agent behavior.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:8969:- **Recommendation**: NEW FEATURE
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:8970:- **Impact**: high
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:8972:### Finding 3: Modus has an explicit **trust-stage autonomy control plane**
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:8974:- **What it does**: Modus persists `atlas/trust.md` with stage `1/2/3` (`Inform`, `Recommend`, `Act`), defaults safely to stage 1, records transition history in the body, and exposes MCP get/set tools while explicitly stating the system never self-promotes. In the Public memory files reviewed, I did not find an equivalent global autonomy mode; reconsolidation is advisory/shadow-only or narrow auto-archive behavior, not policy-level permissioning.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:8975:- **Why it matters for us**: Public already has powerful memory mutation and recommendation paths. A single operator-set autonomy state could gate when memory-derived suggestions remain advisory versus when certain maintenance or consolidation actions may execute automatically.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:8976:- **Recommendation**: NEW FEATURE
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:8977:- **Impact**: high
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:8979:### Finding 4: Modus models **belief change as proposals with operator adjudication**
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:8981:- **What it does**: Modus can open a PR-like markdown proposal against an `entity`, `belief`, or `fact`, store reasoning and linked belief IDs, then on merge reinforce linked beliefs or on rejection weaken them. Public’s reconsolidation bridge does similarity-based archive/review suggestions, but the reviewed path has no durable pending-proposal object, no explicit operator merge/reject event, and no confidence side-effects tied to that decision.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:8982:- **Why it matters for us**: This is a governance feature, not a search feature. It would give Public a clean way to handle contested or evolving memories where “keep/merge/archive” is too small a decision surface.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:8983:- **Recommendation**: NEW FEATURE
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:8984:- **Impact**: high
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:8986:### Finding 5: Modus couples decay to a **stale-memory quarantine workflow**
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:8988:- **What it does**: Modus decays fact confidence, then `memory_archive_stale` marks low-confidence facts `archived=true` with `archived_at`; the fact indexer maps archived facts to `IsActive=0`, and fact search excludes inactive items. Public supports archived state and opt-in `trackAccess`, but in the reviewed code those are not tied into an automatic “low confidence -> quarantine from retrieval” sweep.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:8989:- **Why it matters for us**: Public already has richer decay and archive concepts, but the missing piece is a policy-driven cleanup loop that turns decay into an operational retrieval boundary. That would reduce search clutter without deleting history.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:8990:- **Recommendation**: NEW FEATURE
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:8991:- **Impact**: medium
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:8993:### Finding 6: Modus has a lightweight **entity/belief atlas** on top of markdown, not just generic memories
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:8995:- **What it does**: Modus separates `atlas/entities/` and `atlas/beliefs/`, resolves `[[wiki-links]]`, exposes entity pages with resolved links, lists beliefs by subject, and applies predicate-specific confidence decay to belief records. In the Public memory surface reviewed, the exposed tools are retrieval/context/save/trigger oriented; I did not find a first-class entity/belief object model or wiki-link-resolved atlas layer.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:8996:- **Why it matters for us**: Public already has causal links and code-graph structure, but this would add a human-readable “world model” layer for stable actors, systems, products, or concepts that many memories refer to indirectly.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:8997:- **Recommendation**: prototype later
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:8998:- **Impact**: medium
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:9000:## Sources Consulted
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:9020:## Assessment
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:9025:## Reflection
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:9029:## Recommended Next Focus
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:9042:# Iteration 012: GAP ANALYSIS - REFACTORS
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:9044:## Focus
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:9047:## Findings
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:9048:### Finding 1: **Reject Modus’s fuzzy result-cache architecture; it is the wrong cache contract for Public**
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:9050:- **What it does**: Modus caches full result sets with two tiers: exact query hash and fuzzy reuse when Jaccard similarity between token sets is `>= 0.6`. Its “LRU” bookkeeping is weaker than advertised: `counter` advances only on `put`, while `get` rewrites `hits` without advancing recency, so eviction is really write-recency-biased rather than true read-recency-aware. Public’s cache is a canonical-args SHA256 key with TTL, explicit invalidation by tool, and generation bumps to prevent stale reuse after mutations.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:9051:- **Why it matters for us**: Public’s retrieval result is shaped by many dynamic knobs — scope, archived filtering, session boost, causal boost, rerank, quality thresholds, trace flags, progressive disclosure. Modus-style fuzzy query reuse would blur those dimensions and risk replaying the wrong scored payload for a merely similar query. This is a direction change we should avoid.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:9052:- **Recommendation**: reject
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:9053:- **Impact**: high
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:9055:### Finding 2: **Do not simplify deep-mode query expansion to Modus’s “union then cap” flow**
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:9057:- **What it does**: Modus expands a query into up to 6 strings, runs searches per variant, deduplicates by `path` or `subject|predicate`, then truncates to the requested limit. There is no cross-variant score reconciliation, branch attribution, or post-merge rerank contract; early variants effectively dominate. Public’s deep mode already runs variant branches with timeout protection and `Promise.allSettled`, merges rows by canonical id while preserving branch/source scores, then applies fusion and rerank under a deterministic ranking contract.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:9058:- **Why it matters for us**: Modus’s expansion prompt is interesting, but its merge semantics are weaker than Public’s current pipeline. If we refactor our deep retrieval path toward a simpler variant union, we would lose ranking fidelity and auditability. The right direction is to keep all expansion strategies inside Stage 1 and let Stage 2/3 own scoring.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:9059:- **Recommendation**: reject
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:9060:- **Impact**: high
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:9062:### Finding 3: **Adopt a stricter “one retrieval core” rule; Modus’s duplicated fact search paths are a warning**
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:9064:- **What it does**: Modus has at least two fact-retrieval contracts for the same memory objects: a general document BM25 index filtered to `memory/facts/`, and a separate in-memory `factStore` with its own term index, confidence boost, recency boost, and active-flag handling. `memory_search` uses `Index.SearchFacts`, while `Vault.SearchFacts` uses a different path and fallback behavior. Public, by contrast, pushes `memory_search` through a single pipeline config and result contract.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:9065:- **Why it matters for us**: This is the clearest architecture lesson from Modus for refactor planning: do not create a second “special fast path” retrieval engine for a subset of memories. If Public adds more specialized search entry points, they should stay thin wrappers over the existing pipeline instead of growing parallel ranking rules that will drift.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:9066:- **Recommendation**: adopt now
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:9067:- **Impact**: high
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:9069:### Finding 4: **Refactor Public’s access-writeback out of Stage 2 fusion before expanding it further**
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:9071:- **What it does**: Modus treats every returned fact as a successful recall and asynchronously reinforces it straight from the MCP handler. Public is safer — `trackAccess` is explicit and off by default — but the actual FSRS strengthening write-back still runs inside Stage 2 fusion, alongside scoring signals like session boost, causal boost, and intent weighting.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:9072:- **Why it matters for us**: Comparing the two systems makes the boundary problem obvious: search-time mutation is a different concern from ranking. Public should keep the explicit opt-in policy, but move the write-back/testing-effect path into a post-retrieval access-effects module so Stage 2 stays purely about score construction. That will make future FSRS/review-policy work easier without creeping side effects into ranking logic.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:9073:- **Recommendation**: adopt now
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:9074:- **Impact**: high
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:9076:### Finding 5: **If we want Modus-style “connected docs” ergonomics, add them as a formatter over existing graph evidence, not as a new index**
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:9078:- **What it does**: Modus appends a lightweight connected-doc block after primary results using fixed subject/entity/tag weights. Public already computes and exposes graph contribution metadata in the pipeline response, but it does not currently turn that into a first-class “you may also want these linked memories” presentation block.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:9079:- **Why it matters for us**: The storage/index part of Modus’s adjacency model is not worth adopting over Public’s causal graph. But the UX layer is reusable: a formatter that emits graph-derived related-memory hints could improve browsing without touching retrieval architecture or inventing another relationship store.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:9080:- **Recommendation**: prototype later
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:9081:- **Impact**: medium
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:9083:## Sources Consulted
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:9110:## Assessment
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:9115:## Reflection
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:9119:## Recommended Next Focus
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:9132:# Iteration 013: GAP ANALYSIS - PARADIGM SHIFTS
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:9134:## Focus
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:9137:## Findings
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:9139:### Finding 1: **Modus treats memory as a multi-plane operating system, not one indexed corpus**
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:9141:- **What it does**: Modus splits memory into distinct planes with different lifecycles: `brain/learnings` for cross-model operational lessons, `atlas/prs` for proposal artifacts, `missions/*` for execution state, and `atlas/*` for entity/belief knowledge. `crossref.go` then federates those planes into one neighborhood query without flattening them into one storage contract. Public’s exposed surface is broader in raw tooling, but it is still centered on one generic memory substrate plus adjunct mutation, checkpoint, causal, and pre/postflight tools.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:9142:- **Why it matters for us**: This is the clearest paradigm shift in the codebase. The next architecture step for Public is likely **not another retrieval feature**, but separating “retrieved knowledge,” “operational lessons,” “governed assertions,” and “execution state” into first-class memory planes with different mutation and surfacing rules.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:9143:- **Recommendation**: **NEW FEATURE**
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:9144:- **Impact**: **high**
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:9146:### Finding 2: **Governance should include an explicit autonomy stage, not just access control**
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:9148:- **What it does**: Modus persists a trust stage in `atlas/trust.md`, defaults to stage 1 when absent, forbids self-promotion in code comments and tool descriptions, and appends human-readable transition history on every stage change. The stage changes runtime posture: Inform, Recommend, or Act. Public’s governance surface is strong on tenant/shared-space access and safe mutation boundaries, but that is **scope governance**, not **autonomy governance**.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:9149:- **Why it matters for us**: Public currently decides “can this caller touch this memory?” but not “may the system autonomously execute, only recommend, or only observe?” Modus suggests adding an operator-owned autonomy layer above mutation tools, reconsolidation actions, and future approval workflows.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:9150:- **Recommendation**: **prototype later**
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:9151:- **Impact**: **high**
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:9153:### Finding 3: **High-risk memory changes should pass through a durable proposal inbox**
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:9155:- **What it does**: Modus opens persistent PR artifacts with `target_type`, `target_id`, `reasoning`, `confidence`, and `linked_belief_ids`; merge/reject is explicit and feeds back into belief confidence. Public already has the raw ingredients for this pattern: assistive reconsolidation classifies borderline pairs and emits review-tier recommendations, but today those remain console warnings while actual changes still happen via direct `memory_update` / `memory_delete` / bulk-delete flows.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:9156:- **Why it matters for us**: This is an immediately actionable shift. Public does not need Modus’s whole atlas model to benefit; it can turn its existing shadow recommendations into durable proposal rows with accept/reject outcomes, creating a real operator inbox for reconsolidation, supersession, and possibly promotion/deprecation decisions.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:9157:- **Recommendation**: **adopt now**
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:9158:- **Impact**: **high**
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:9160:### Finding 4: **Decay should drive visible workflow states, not stay buried inside scoring and cleanup**
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:9162:- **What it does**: In Modus, recall and review outcomes change named artifact state directly: facts reinforce and later archive, beliefs decay/reinforce/weaken, learnings deprecate by zeroing confidence, and PR outcomes feed back into linked beliefs. Public has equivalent mechanics scattered across hidden layers: `trackAccess` is opt-in and off by default, FSRS write-back lives inside Stage 2, batch learning is explicitly shadow-only, negative validation adjusts scoring confidence, and archival runs as a background subsystem.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:9163:- **Why it matters for us**: The shift is from “decay as ranking math” to “decay as memory operations workflow.” Public already has most primitives, but not a first-class stale/review/promote/deprecate surface that operators can inspect and triage. That missing workflow layer is now a bigger gap than the decay math itself.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:9164:- **Recommendation**: **NEW FEATURE**
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:9165:- **Impact**: **high**
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:9167:### Finding 5: **Memory can become an execution-readiness layer, not just a recall layer**
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:9169:- **What it does**: Modus stores missions as markdown objects with typed dependencies (`blocks`, `informs`, `enhances`), cycle checks, satisfaction status, and `CanStart` readiness logic surfaced through MCP. Connected search can then return missions alongside facts, beliefs, and learnings. Public’s nearest analogue is `task_preflight` / `task_postflight`, but those record epistemic telemetry after or before work; they do not create a live ready/blocked execution graph.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:9170:- **Why it matters for us**: This suggests a second major shift: memory should not only help answer questions; it should also expose what work is blocked, what is ready, and what knowledge dependencies remain unresolved. That would pair naturally with the proposal inbox and review queue ideas.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:9171:- **Recommendation**: **prototype later**
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:9172:- **Impact**: **medium**
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:9174:## Sources Consulted
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:9199:## Assessment
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:9204:## Reflection
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:9208:## Recommended Next Focus
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:9221:# Iteration 014: DEEP DIVE - STRONGEST PATTERN
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:9223:## Focus
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:9226:## Findings
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:9228:### Finding 1: **Modus’s strongest pattern is a durable proposal inbox implemented as first-class markdown artifacts**
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:9230:- **What it does**: Modus persists each risky knowledge change as `atlas/prs/<slug>.md`, not as transient handler state. `OpenPR()` writes frontmatter with `title`, `opened_by`, `status=open`, `opened_at`, `target_type`, `target_id`, `confidence`, and `linked_belief_ids`, plus a markdown reasoning body. The generic vault read/write path means PRs use the same plain-file contract as the rest of the system: human-readable, git-diffable, and operator-editable.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:9231:- **Why it matters for us**: This is the most valuable pattern in the repo because it turns memory mutation review into a durable operating surface instead of console output or ephemeral response metadata. Public already has strong mutation safety, but not a persistent review object that can be listed, approved, rejected, or resumed later.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:9232:- **Recommendation**: **NEW FEATURE**
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:9233:- **Impact**: **high**
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:9235:### Finding 2: **The PR lifecycle is decision-tracking plus belief feedback, not target mutation execution**
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:9237:- **What it does**: On merge, Modus marks the PR `merged`, stamps `closed_at`/`closed_by`, and reinforces each `linked_belief_id`; on reject, it marks `rejected`, stores `rejection_reason`, and weakens linked beliefs. Critically, `target_type` and `target_id` are recorded when the PR is opened, but the merge/reject path does **not** apply any mutation to that target object. The proposal is an evidence/governance artifact whose outcome feeds confidence updates into beliefs.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:9238:- **Why it matters for us**: This is the key end-to-end nuance. The best part of the pattern is not “PRs for memories”; it is “operator decisions become durable signals that strengthen or weaken the system’s confidence model.” Public should copy that feedback loop, not assume proposals themselves are the mutation engine.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:9239:- **Recommendation**: **adopt now**
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:9240:- **Impact**: **high**
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:9242:### Finding 3: **The trust-stage model is real state, but in current code it is governance metadata, not enforced runtime gating**
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:9244:- **What it does**: Modus persists `atlas/trust.md`, defaults to stage 1 when absent, records transition history in the markdown body, and exposes MCP tools to get/set the stage with labels `Inform`, `Recommend`, and `Act`. But the current code does not use that stage to gate `atlas_open_pr`, `atlas_merge_pr`, `atlas_reject_pr`, or other mutation paths.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:9245:- **Why it matters for us**: This corrects the earlier high-level read: the trust system is an operator-owned posture marker, but not yet a hard control plane. For Public, copying it verbatim would add a policy label without safety value unless it is wired into mutation and approval paths.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:9246:- **Recommendation**: **reject**
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:9247:- **Impact**: **medium**
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:9249:### Finding 4: **This proposal workflow is not part of Modus’s default memory-only surface; it lives in the broader vault control plane**
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:9251:- **What it does**: `RegisterMemoryTools()` trims the exposed toolset down to 11 search/memory tools and removes the PR/trust tools from that profile. The proposal inbox exists in the broader vault tool registration, not in the lean memory profile described in the README.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:9252:- **Why it matters for us**: That makes the pattern more credible, not less: even Modus treats it as an advanced control-plane layer above retrieval, not as core search behavior. Public should do the same and avoid entangling proposal workflow with the canonical retrieval interface.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:9253:- **Recommendation**: **prototype later**
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:9254:- **Impact**: **medium**
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:9256:### Finding 5: **Public has auditability and advisory signals, but still lacks the durable triage object that makes Modus’s pattern useful**
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:9258:- **What it does**: Public’s review-tier reconsolidation only creates an `assistiveRecommendation` payload and console warning; it is forwarded in the `memory_save` response but not persisted as a reviewable object. Meanwhile `memory_update`, `memory_delete`, and `memory_bulk_delete` act directly, protected by transactionality, checkpoints, and the append-only mutation ledger. `memory_validate` updates confidence, negative-feedback state, and promotion eligibility, but only emits hints like “consider updating or deleting.”
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:9259:- **Why it matters for us**: Public’s gap is no longer retrieval math or mutation safety. The missing layer is a persistent operator inbox that can hold borderline reconsolidation, supersession, promotion, or deprecation proposals and then feed accept/reject outcomes back into existing confidence and feedback systems.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:9260:- **Recommendation**: **NEW FEATURE**
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:9261:- **Impact**: **high**
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:9263:## Sources Consulted
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:9283:## Assessment
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:9288:## Reflection
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:9292:## Recommended Next Focus
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:9305:# Iteration 015: DEEP DIVE - SECOND PATTERN
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:9307:## Focus
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:9310:## Findings
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:9312:### Finding 1: **Modus treats successful recall as a default write-on-read event**
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:9314:- **What it does**: `memory_search` merges fact hits across librarian-expanded queries, then asynchronously calls `go v.ReinforceFact(f.ID)` for every returned fact. `MemFact.ID` is the relative markdown path loaded at index time, so the search result is directly wired back to the underlying file. `ReinforceFact()` then raises confidence toward `0.99`, grows `stability`, lowers `difficulty`, stamps `last_accessed`, and increments `access_count`.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:9315:- **Why it matters for us**: This is the core of Modus’s second pattern: recall is not just ranking telemetry, it is a lifecycle event that mutates memory state by default. Public already has the underlying primitive, but keeps it deliberately opt-in via `trackAccess` rather than making every search a write.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:9316:- **Recommendation**: **prototype later**
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:9317:- **Impact**: **high**
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:9319:### Finding 2: **Modus exposes decay, reinforcement, and archival as an operator-facing memory hygiene loop**
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:9321:- **What it does**: Fact decay is a first-class maintenance action (`memory_decay_facts`), archival is a first-class cleanup action (`memory_archive_stale`), and reinforcement is both implicit (`memory_search`) and explicit (`memory_reinforce`). The FSRS state lives in markdown frontmatter (`confidence`, `stability`, `difficulty`, `last_accessed`, `access_count`, `archived`), so operators can inspect and edit the lifecycle directly.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:9322:- **Why it matters for us**: Public has stronger internals than Modus here, but not the same explicit operating surface. Our FSRS, archival, and feedback systems mostly live behind retrieval and storage handlers; Modus shows the value of turning memory freshness into a visible maintenance workflow.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:9323:- **Recommendation**: **NEW FEATURE**
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:9324:- **Impact**: **high**
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:9326:### Finding 3: **The visible “stale” surface in Modus is useful, but its actual scheduling heuristic is much cruder than it looks**
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:9328:- **What it does**: Search ranking uses simple age buckets from `createdAt` (`hot`, `warm`, `recent`, `cold`), and result rendering appends `⚠ >30d old` / `⚠ >90d old` warnings from `StalenessWarning()`. Those warnings are based on creation age, not `last_accessed`, `stability`, `difficulty`, or a real due-review timestamp. Archival is then driven by a confidence threshold, not a due queue.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:9329:- **Why it matters for us**: The surface is good; the heuristic is not. Public should not copy this exact implementation because our FSRS stack can already express a more truthful notion of “due,” “cold,” or “review-needed” than crude age bands.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:9330:- **Recommendation**: **reject**
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:9331:- **Impact**: **medium**
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:9333:### Finding 4: **Public already has richer decay/governance primitives than Modus, but they are intentionally hidden behind safer defaults**
