/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:9335:- **What it does**: Public’s `memory_search` defaults `trackAccess=false`, and Stage 2 only applies FSRS strengthening when that opt-in flag is set. `memory_validate` records positive/negative usefulness, adaptive signals, auto-promotion, learned feedback, and ground-truth selection. Batch learning is shadow-only, and archival uses protected tiers plus age/access/confidence filters. In other words, Public already has the mechanics Modus needs several explicit tools to approximate.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:9336:- **Why it matters for us**: The gap is no longer retrieval math or decay correctness. The gap is that Public’s stronger primitives are distributed across internals and safety rails, while Modus packages a simpler subset as an obvious operator workflow.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:9337:- **Recommendation**: **reject**
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:9338:- **Impact**: **high**
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:9340:### Finding 5: **The best transfer is a thin review/due-items layer on top of Public’s existing systems—not Modus’s exact fact model**
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:9342:- **What it does**: Public already exposes session readiness (`session_health`, `session_bootstrap`) and task-learning analytics (`task_preflight`, `task_postflight`), but not a comparable operator surface for memory hygiene. The right abstraction is a dedicated queue for memories that are due for review, repeatedly unhelpful, promotion-eligible, or archival candidates—fed by existing FSRS, validation, and archival signals.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:9343:- **Why it matters for us**: This is the concrete implementation delta. Modus proves the value of an operator-visible control plane, but Public should build that plane from its own stronger state model rather than regress to Modus’s simpler markdown-fact lifecycle.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:9344:- **Recommendation**: **NEW FEATURE**
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:9345:- **Impact**: **high**
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:9347:## Sources Consulted
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:9366:## Assessment
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:9371:## Reflection
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:9375:## Recommended Next Focus
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:9388:# Iteration 016: EDGE CASES & FAILURE MODES
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:9390:## Focus
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:9393:## Findings
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:9395:### Finding 1: **Modus’s “degraded mode” is not actually survivable**
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:9397:- **What it does**: Startup logs `index build failed` and claims it can continue, but `main.go` still calls `idx.DocCount()` even when `index.Build()` returned `nil, err`. The MCP layer also assumes an index exists: `memory_search` calls `v.Index.SearchFacts(...)` directly, while only the lower-level `Vault.SearchFacts()` method contains a no-index fallback. `vault_connected` also just returns `"Index not loaded."` rather than recovering.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:9398:- **Why it matters for us**: This is a brittle failure contract: one bad scan can turn “start degraded” into a startup panic or nil-deref path. Public is already stricter here: DB refresh/rebind is mutexed, refuses empty-db drift, and returns explicit degraded/error responses instead of pretending recovery succeeded.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:9399:- **Recommendation**: **reject**
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:9400:- **Impact**: **high**
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:9402:### Finding 2: **Parser corruption is silently normalized instead of surfaced**
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:9404:- **What it does**: If YAML frontmatter fails to parse, `Parse()` silently treats the frontmatter block as body text and returns success. `ScanDir()` also suppresses filesystem walk errors and parse failures (`return nil // skip errors`, `return nil // skip unparseable files`). Then `doctor` operates on that already-sanitized view, so malformed frontmatter is reduced to softer symptoms like “documents without frontmatter” or missing fact fields rather than a hard corruption report.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:9405:- **Why it matters for us**: Modus optimizes for “keep indexing something,” but the cost is silent data quality loss and weaker operator trust. Public’s `memory_save` path is materially safer: preflight, dry-run, validation, and atomic save all give us a better place to reject malformed state before it becomes indexed ambiguity.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:9406:- **Recommendation**: **reject**
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:9407:- **Impact**: **high**
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:9409:### Finding 3: **The plain-file write and migration path is non-atomic, partial, and lossy**
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:9411:- **What it does**: Core writes go straight to the final path with `os.WriteFile`; Khoj import uses `os.Create` directly, continues after per-file failures, skips already-existing files, truncates imported context over 2000 chars, deduplicates context by the first 200 chars, and falls back to `time.Now()` when timestamps cannot be parsed. There is no temp-file promotion, rollback, checkpoint, manifest, or resume journal.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:9412:- **Why it matters for us**: This is fine for a lightweight local tool, but it is the wrong mutation model for governed or high-value memory. Public already has the stronger pattern: checkpointed bulk operations, rollback-aware atomic saves, and post-insert cleanup when metadata application fails. If we ever add importer workflows or operator batch jobs, they should follow Public’s mutation model, not Modus’s write-through importer.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:9413:- **Recommendation**: **reject**
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:9414:- **Impact**: **high**
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:9416:### Finding 4: **A timestamp edge case can disable the intended memory lifecycle**
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:9418:- **What it does**: `StoreFact()` writes `created: "now"` as a literal string. But recency boosting, tier labeling, staleness warnings, and decay all expect parseable dates or RFC3339 timestamps. That means newly stored facts can miss age-aware behavior until some later successful recall writes `last_accessed`. Separately, Khoj import converts bad timestamps to the current wall clock, which silently rewrites chronology.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:9419:- **Why it matters for us**: This is a good example of how lightweight markdown persistence can quietly undermine lifecycle logic if timestamps are not normalized at write time. Public should keep lifecycle timestamps machine-parseable and validation-gated at ingest; our current preflight/save architecture already gives us the right enforcement point.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:9420:- **Recommendation**: **reject**
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:9421:- **Impact**: **medium**
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:9423:### Finding 5: **Scaling is “rebuild everything in memory,” with a few latent concurrency footguns**
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:9425:- **What it does**: Every build/open rescans markdown, reparses documents, rebuilds BM25, cross-refs, and the fact store entirely in RAM. Prefix fallback linearly scans the full term map. `memory_search` launches a goroutine per returned fact for asynchronous reinforcement writes. The cache uses `RWMutex`, but `get()` mutates `hits` while holding only `RLock`, which is unsafe if request handling ever becomes concurrent. The current stdio loop is serial, so that race is partly masked today.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:9426:- **Why it matters for us**: Modus’s architecture is intentionally simple, but it does not transfer well to Public’s larger, longer-lived memory surface. Public’s incremental DB-backed indexing, guarded rebind path, and post-restore rebuild hooks are the right direction; the Modus lesson is mostly where the scaling cliffs are, not a backend we should copy.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:9427:- **Recommendation**: **reject**
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:9428:- **Impact**: **high**
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:9430:### Finding 6: **The strongest transferable pattern here is the operator-facing “doctor,” not Modus’s repair depth**
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:9432:- **What it does**: Modus gives operators a very readable `health`/`doctor` surface: counts, contradictions, duplicates, empty docs, directory distribution. But it is mostly advisory; it does not pair diagnosis with durable rollback or strong repair flows. Public already has deeper repair machinery—auto-repair for FTS drift, orphan-edge cleanup, restore barriers, checkpoint restore index rebuilds—but the surface is more technical and less “single-screen triage.”
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:9433:- **Why it matters for us**: The net-new idea is not Modus’s recovery engine; it is the usability of a compact operator diagnostic surface. Public could benefit from a friendlier “doctor summary” layered over existing `memory_health`, checkpoints, and mutation ledgers.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:9434:- **Recommendation**: **NEW FEATURE**
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:9435:- **Impact**: **medium**
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:9437:## Sources Consulted
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:9465:## Assessment
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:9470:## Reflection
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:9474:## Recommended Next Focus
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:9487:# Iteration 017: INTEGRATION FEASIBILITY
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:9489:## Focus
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:9492:## Findings
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:9494:### Finding 1: **Medium integration** — operator-visible review queue is the highest-value Modus pattern
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:9496:- **What it does**: Modus makes the full decay loop visible: `memory_search` reinforces recalled facts, `memory_decay_facts` runs the sweep, `memory_archive_stale` archives low-confidence facts, `memory_reinforce` lets an operator review a fact directly, and result formatting exposes tier/staleness cues. Public already has the harder backend pieces: FSRS columns in `memory_index`, canonical review math, strengthening-on-access, and archival machinery. The missing piece is a first-class due/review queue.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:9497:- **Why it matters for us**: This is the clearest “control-plane gap.” Integration is **medium**, not high, because Public does **not** need new decay math or new storage; it needs a new MCP surface that turns existing `stability`, `difficulty`, `last_review`, `review_count`, archival state, and promotion signals into an actionable inbox.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:9498:- **Recommendation**: **NEW FEATURE**
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:9499:- **Impact**: **high**
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:9501:### Finding 2: **Low integration** — a doctor-style summary fits Public almost immediately
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:9503:- **What it does**: Modus’s `doctor` composes one-screen triage from existing index state: counts, missing fact fields, duplicate subject/predicate pairs, contradictions, empty docs, and directory distribution. Public already has deeper primitives than Modus—health diagnostics, FTS repair, orphan-edge cleanup, checkpoint restore, and post-restore index rebuilds—but they are exposed as richer technical envelopes rather than a compact operator summary.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:9504:- **Why it matters for us**: Integration is **low** because the data is already there. This is mostly formatter/reporting work: a friendlier “doctor” mode layered on `memory_health`, not a new subsystem.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:9505:- **Recommendation**: **adopt now**
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:9506:- **Impact**: **medium**
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:9508:### Finding 3: **Medium integration** — Modus-style lexical expansion can reuse Public’s deep-mode hooks
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:9510:- **What it does**: Modus expands one query into 3-5 lexical variants, runs retrieval per variant, and merges deduped results. Public already has three nearby hooks: corpus-grounded LLM reformulation, deep-mode query decomposition, and graph-expanded fallback queries. So integration is not about inventing expansion; it is about deciding whether a **lighter lexical-only branch** should run before or beside the current deep pipeline.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:9511:- **Why it matters for us**: Integration is **medium** because the insertion points already exist, but the value is incremental, not foundational. Public must avoid duplicating its current deep-mode expansion stack or adding noisy variant fan-out across vector, FTS, BM25, and graph lanes.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:9512:- **Recommendation**: **prototype later**
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:9513:- **Impact**: **medium**
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:9515:### Finding 4: **Medium integration** — connected-doc hints are feasible, but should stay explanatory first
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:9517:- **What it does**: Modus builds an in-memory metadata adjacency map from subject/tag/entity overlap, weights subject/entity/tag at `3/2/1`, and appends “connected” documents that were not in the main ranked set. Public already has stronger structural enrichment—causal-neighbor boost, neighbor injection, and co-activation spreading—but those are graph-driven, not simple frontmatter co-membership summaries.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:9518:- **Why it matters for us**: Integration is **medium**. Public should not replace causal machinery with Modus’s simpler adjacency model, but it could add a lightweight explanation layer: “related by shared subject/tag/entity” as an appendix or secondary lane. That would improve operator comprehension without perturbing the main ranking path too aggressively.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:9519:- **Recommendation**: **prototype later**
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:9520:- **Impact**: **medium**
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:9522:### Finding 5: **Low integration** — Public can expose explicit review/reinforce actions without enabling global write-on-read
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:9524:- **What it does**: Modus exposes both automatic reinforcement on recall and an explicit `memory_reinforce` tool. Public already has the underlying grade model (`AGAIN/HARD/GOOD/EASY`), review processing, and strengthening-on-access logic, but keeps write-back behind `trackAccess=false` and has no first-class `memory_reinforce`/`memory_review` tool in the current schema set.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:9525:- **Why it matters for us**: Integration is **low** because the math and columns already exist. The work is mostly safe API design: expose explicit review as a deliberate operator action, instead of flipping Public toward Modus-style default write-on-read.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:9526:- **Recommendation**: **adopt now**
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:9527:- **Impact**: **medium**
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:9529:## Sources Consulted
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:9559:## Assessment
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:9564:## Reflection
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:9568:## Recommended Next Focus
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:9581:# Iteration 018: RISK ASSESSMENT
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:9583:## Focus
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:9586:## Findings
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:9588:### Finding 1: Review queue risk — a Public `memory_due` inbox will be misleading unless it gets a first-class due-state contract
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:9590:- **What it does**: Modus keeps search recall, explicit reinforce, decay, and archive on the same fact fields. Public already persists `access_count`, `last_accessed`, `stability`, `difficulty`, `last_review`, and `review_count`, and `processReview()` computes `nextReviewDate`, but that due date is not persisted and read-time strengthening stays opt-in via `trackAccess=false`.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:9591:- **Why it matters for us**: If Public adds a due/review queue without defining one authoritative source of truth for “due,” the queue can drift from actual search behavior. A memory can look overdue while recent read activity updated its effective retrievability, which would make the inbox feel unreliable.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:9592:- **Recommendation**: **NEW FEATURE**
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:9593:- **Impact**: **high**
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:9595:### Finding 2: Doctor summary risk — a friendlier `doctor` surface can hide the repair semantics that currently keep Public safe
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:9597:- **What it does**: Modus’s `doctor` is compact: build the index, count issues, print warnings. Public’s health path is richer: degraded/healthy distinction, confirmation-gated auto-repair, FTS/vector/orphan cleanup, and partial-restore error reporting.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:9598:- **Why it matters for us**: The risk is lossy compression. If Public promotes a one-screen doctor summary as the main surface, operators may miss whether a problem is merely informational, auto-repairable, or a partial restore failure with rollback details.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:9599:- **Recommendation**: **adopt now**
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:9600:- **Impact**: **medium**
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:9602:### Finding 3: Lexical expansion risk — copying Modus’s librarian directly would stack another weak branch onto Public’s already-expanded deep pipeline
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:9604:- **What it does**: Modus asks an LLM for 3-5 keyword variants with no corpus grounding and searches each variant. Public already has deep-mode decomposition, corpus-grounded reformulation, and graph-expanded fallback queries.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:9605:- **Why it matters for us**: In Modus this is lightweight; in Public it risks branch explosion, extra latency, and noisier recall. Ungrounded lexical variants can duplicate or fight with stronger grounded/vector lanes, especially when reranking and graph fallback already exist.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:9606:- **Recommendation**: **prototype later**
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:9607:- **Impact**: **medium**
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:9609:### Finding 4: Connected-doc hint risk — metadata adjacency is useful as explanation, but risky as a scoring signal
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:9611:- **What it does**: Modus builds an in-memory adjacency map from subject/tag/entity overlap with weights `3/2/1`, using lightweight matching. Public already has causal traversal, neighbor injection, and co-activation enrichment.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:9612:- **Why it matters for us**: The risk is precision loss. Substring entity matches and coarse tag overlap can create false positives or duplicate relationships already expressed by Public’s graph layers. If those hints affect ranking, they can pollute better hybrid results.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:9613:- **Recommendation**: **prototype later**
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:9614:- **Impact**: **medium**
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:9616:### Finding 5: Explicit review action risk — a success-only `memory_reinforce` would undercut Public’s richer FSRS vocabulary
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:9618:- **What it does**: Modus exposes a success-only reinforce action: stability up, difficulty slightly down, confidence nudged toward 1.0, access count incremented. Public’s scheduler already supports `AGAIN/HARD/GOOD/EASY`, but the exposed read path only offers opt-in GOOD-style strengthening through `trackAccess`.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:9619:- **Why it matters for us**: If Public copies Modus literally, every explicit review starts looking like successful recall. That biases histories upward and makes any later due/review queue less trustworthy because “hard” or failed recall events have no first-class path.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:9620:- **Recommendation**: **adopt now**
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:9621:- **Impact**: **medium**
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:9623:## Sources Consulted
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:9645:## Assessment
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:9650:## Reflection
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:9654:## Recommended Next Focus
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:9667:# Iteration 019: PRIORITY RANKING
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:9669:## Focus
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:9672:## Findings
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:9674:### Finding 1: Rank 1 (20/25) — explicit `memory_review` is the first thing Public should ship
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:9676:- **What it does**: Modus exposes an explicit `memory_reinforce` operator action on top of its FSRS state. Public already has the underlying pieces: graded review math (`AGAIN/HARD/GOOD/EASY`), `processReview()` with `nextReviewDate`, and opt-in strengthening-on-access. The current exposed adjacent surface is `memory_validate`, which records only `wasUseful` feedback and does not let an operator issue a graded FSRS review.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:9677:- **Why it matters for us**: This is the highest impact x feasibility transfer because it closes the clearest control-plane gap without inventing new storage or changing retrieval architecture. It also de-risks the bigger `memory_due` idea: until Public has a first-class graded review action, any future review queue would have no trustworthy way to record hard recalls, lapses, and easy wins distinctly.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:9678:- **Recommendation**: **adopt now**
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:9679:- **Impact**: **high**
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:9681:### Finding 2: Rank 2 (15/25, tie-broken ahead) — a doctor-style summary should ship in the same tranche as `memory_review`
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:9683:- **What it does**: Modus turns index and vault diagnostics into a one-screen operator summary. Public already has the deeper machinery: `memory_health` with confirmation-gated auto-repair, FTS rebuild, orphan-edge cleanup, orphan-vector cleanup, and degraded/healthy reporting. The gap is presentation, not backend capability.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:9684:- **Why it matters for us**: This is nearly pure formatter work and improves operator trust immediately. It ranks ahead of `memory_due` despite lower raw strategic upside because it is safer, independent, and can ship without any FSRS contract decisions.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:9685:- **Recommendation**: **adopt now**
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:9686:- **Impact**: **medium**
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:9688:### Finding 3: Rank 3 (15/25, tie-broken behind) — `memory_due` is the biggest strategic win, but only after a due-state contract is fixed
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:9690:- **What it does**: Modus exposes the full review lifecycle visibly: search recall, explicit reinforce, decay, and archive all operate over the same fact state. Public already stores `stability`, `difficulty`, `last_review`, `review_count`, and `last_accessed`, and `processReview()` computes `nextReviewDate`, but no persisted `next_review_at` column exists and write-on-read remains opt-in through `trackAccess=false`.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:9691:- **Why it matters for us**: This is the most valuable missing product surface, but it should not ship first. If Public adds a queue before deciding whether “due” is persisted (`next_review_at`) or deterministically derived from `stability + last_review`, operators will see a review inbox that can drift from actual FSRS behavior.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:9692:- **Recommendation**: **NEW FEATURE**
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:9693:- **Impact**: **high**
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:9695:### Finding 4: Rank 4 (9/25) — connected-doc hints are worth prototyping only as an explanation lane
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:9697:- **What it does**: Modus builds a lightweight adjacency layer from subject/tag/entity overlap and appends “connected docs not in results above” as an extra hint surface. Public already has stronger graph-backed enrichment via causal boosts, neighbor injection, and co-activation, but not a simple metadata-derived explanation appendix.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:9698:- **Why it matters for us**: The feasible transfer is not ranking logic; it is result explanation. That makes this a useful prototype for operator trust, but not a top roadmap item. If it affects scoring too early, it risks duplicating or diluting better graph evidence already present in Public.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:9699:- **Recommendation**: **prototype later**
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:9700:- **Impact**: **medium**
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:9702:### Finding 5: Rank 5 (6/25) — Modus-style librarian expansion belongs last, and only as weak-result recovery
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:9704:- **What it does**: Modus uses an LLM to emit 3-5 lexical variants, then merges deduped fact results across those variants. Public already has deep-mode reformulation, corpus-seeded variants, and graph-expanded recovery paths; its remaining gap is not “can expand queries,” but “when is an extra lexical branch worth the latency and noise.”
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:9705:- **Why it matters for us**: This has the weakest impact x feasibility score because it overlaps the most with existing Public machinery and carries the highest risk of branch explosion. The only credible adoption path is a bounded fallback for weak-result cases, never an always-on copy of Modus’s librarian path.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:9706:- **Recommendation**: **prototype later**
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:9707:- **Impact**: **low**
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:9709:### Finding 6: The concrete roadmap should follow dependency order, not just raw desirability
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:9711:- **What it does**: The ranking resolves into a four-phase roadmap: **Phase 1** ship `memory_review` plus a doctor-style summary; **Phase 2** define the authoritative due-state contract and then add `memory_due`; **Phase 3** pilot connected-doc explanations as a non-scoring appendix; **Phase 4** run a feature-flagged lexical-expansion experiment only on weak-result fallback paths.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:9712:- **Why it matters for us**: This sequence matches the real code dependencies. Phase 1 uses existing Public primitives with low integration risk. Phase 2 is the first place schema and lifecycle semantics matter. Phases 3-4 stay explicitly out of the main ranking path until they prove incremental value over current causal and deep-mode behavior.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:9713:- **Recommendation**: **adopt now**
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:9714:- **Impact**: **high**
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:9716:## Sources Consulted
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:9740:## Assessment
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:9745:## Reflection
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:9747:- What did not work: The phase packet does not currently contain the reducer-owned deep-research state files, so the ranking had to be reconstructed from prior iteration reports plus source evidence instead of reading a canonical strategy/dashboard packet. That did not block the analysis, but it reduced the amount of loop metadata available for convergence-style bookkeeping.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:9749:## Recommended Next Focus
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:9762:# Iteration 020: FINAL SYNTHESIS
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:9764:## Focus
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:9769:## Findings
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:9771:### Finding 1: Explicit graded review is the best immediate transfer
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:9773:- **What it does**: Modus exposes `memory_reinforce`, but only as a success-only reinforce action. Public already has the stronger primitive: graded FSRS review (`AGAIN/HARD/GOOD/EASY`) plus computed `nextReviewDate`, yet the exposed adjacent tool is only `memory_validate(wasUseful)`, which adjusts confidence rather than recording a real review event.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:9774:- **Why it matters for us**: This closes the clearest control-plane gap without changing retrieval architecture. It also makes any later review queue trustworthy because review history would record hard recalls and lapses, not only “useful / not useful.”
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:9775:- **Recommendation**: adopt now
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:9776:- **Impact**: high
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:9778:### Finding 2: A doctor-style summary should ship beside review
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:9780:- **What it does**: Modus’s `doctor` turns vault health into a one-screen operator report. Public already has deeper health and repair machinery—FTS rebuild, orphan cleanup, degraded/healthy state, confirmation-gated repair—but exposes it as a richer technical envelope.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:9781:- **Why it matters for us**: This is mostly presentation work, not backend work. It improves operator trust immediately while preserving Public’s stronger repair semantics underneath.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:9782:- **Recommendation**: adopt now
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:9783:- **Impact**: medium
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:9785:### Finding 3: Keep one retrieval core; Modus’s split fact-search paths are a warning
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:9787:- **What it does**: Modus has multiple fact-search contracts for the same memory objects: `Index.SearchFacts`, a separate in-memory `factStore`, and `Vault.SearchFacts` fallback behavior. Public routes `memory_search` through one pipeline contract via `executePipeline`.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:9788:- **Why it matters for us**: This is the strongest architectural lesson from Modus’s weaknesses. Public should keep specialized search surfaces as thin wrappers over the canonical pipeline instead of growing parallel ranking rules that will drift.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:9789:- **Recommendation**: adopt now
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:9790:- **Impact**: high
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:9792:### Finding 4: `memory_due` is real product value, but it needs an authoritative due-state contract first
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:9794:- **What it does**: Modus visibly ties search recall, reinforce, decay, and archive to one fact state. Public already stores `stability`, `difficulty`, `last_review`, `review_count`, and opt-in access effects, and computes `nextReviewDate`, but does not expose an operator-facing due queue.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:9795:- **Why it matters for us**: This is the biggest missing workflow surface in Public. But it should not ship until “due” is defined as either persisted state (`next_review_at`) or a deterministic derivation from existing FSRS fields.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:9796:- **Recommendation**: NEW FEATURE
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:9797:- **Impact**: high
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:9799:### Finding 5: Durable proposal inboxes are the best non-retrieval idea Modus has
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:9801:- **What it does**: Modus persists risky knowledge changes as markdown PR artifacts, then merge/reject outcomes reinforce or weaken linked beliefs. Public already produces advisory reconsolidation recommendations and confidence feedback, but the recommendation is transient and not stored as a durable review object.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:9802:- **Why it matters for us**: Public’s next gap is operator workflow, not mutation safety. A persistent inbox for reconsolidation, supersession, promotion, and deprecation proposals would let existing confidence and mutation systems work as a real review loop.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:9803:- **Recommendation**: NEW FEATURE
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:9804:- **Impact**: high
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:9806:### Finding 6: Connected-doc hints are worth prototyping only as an explanation appendix
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:9808:- **What it does**: Modus builds lightweight subject/entity/tag adjacency and appends connected docs that did not make the main ranked list. The weighting is simple and fixed (`subject 3`, `entity 2`, `tag 1`).
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:9809:- **Why it matters for us**: The transferable part is explanation, not ranking. Public can test a “related memories” appendix without diluting stronger graph-backed or hybrid scoring paths.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:9810:- **Recommendation**: prototype later
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:9811:- **Impact**: medium
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:9813:### Finding 7: Lexical-only expansion belongs only in weak-result recovery
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:9815:- **What it does**: Modus asks the Librarian for 3-5 keyword variants, searches each, deduplicates, and caps results. It is lightweight, but ungrounded and union-first.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:9816:- **Why it matters for us**: Public already has a more complex retrieval pipeline, so copying this as a mainline branch would mostly add noise and latency. The only credible transfer is a tightly bounded fallback when hybrid retrieval is weak.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:9817:- **Recommendation**: prototype later
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:9818:- **Impact**: low
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:9820:### Finding 8: Content-level contradiction and duplicate-fact linting is still worth a prototype
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:9822:- **What it does**: Modus’s `doctor` explicitly flags missing `subject`/`predicate`, duplicate `subject+predicate` pairs, and contradictory values for the same pair. Public’s current health surface is stronger on infrastructure integrity than on fact-content linting.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:9823:- **Why it matters for us**: This complements Public’s existing infra health checks instead of replacing them. It is one of the few Modus hygiene ideas that adds net-new value.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:9824:- **Recommendation**: prototype later
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:9825:- **Impact**: medium
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:9827:### Finding 9: Reject Modus’s fuzzy Jaccard result-cache contract
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:9829:- **What it does**: Modus reuses cached result sets on exact hash hits or Jaccard-similar query term sets. Public’s search surface has too many scoring knobs—scope, archived filtering, session/causal boost, rerank, quality thresholds, trace/profile flags—for fuzzy query reuse to be safe.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:9830:- **Why it matters for us**: This is the wrong cache contract for Public. Similar text is not enough to guarantee equivalent retrieval intent or scoring context.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:9831:- **Recommendation**: reject
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:9832:- **Impact**: high
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:9834:### Finding 10: Reject Modus’s permissive markdown parse/write contract
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:9836:- **What it does**: Modus treats malformed YAML as body, skips parse failures during scans, and writes markdown directly with minimal validation. That is good for a personal vault, but it optimizes for permissiveness over authoritative ingestion.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:9837:- **Why it matters for us**: Public’s memory layer is intentionally more governed. Regressing to Modus’s permissive storage contract would weaken memory integrity for little gain.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:9838:- **Recommendation**: reject
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:9839:- **Impact**: high
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:9841:### Finding 11: Reject default write-on-read as Public’s default policy
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:9843:- **What it does**: Modus treats every returned fact as a successful recall and asynchronously reinforces it. Public already supports access effects, but keeps them explicit with `trackAccess=false` by default.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:9844:- **Why it matters for us**: Public’s graded FSRS model is stronger than Modus’s success-only reinforce loop. Default write-on-read would blur retrieval and review, inflate successful-recall history, and make future due-state less trustworthy.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:9845:- **Recommendation**: reject
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:9846:- **Impact**: high
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:9848:### Finding 12: Reject trust-stage metadata unless it becomes real gating
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:9850:- **What it does**: Modus persists trust stage and exposes get/set tools, but current code does not use that state to gate PR or other mutation tools. It is posture metadata, not enforced runtime policy.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:9851:- **Why it matters for us**: Public should not copy symbolic governance. If autonomy stages are added, they must actually gate review, proposal, or mutation flows.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:9852:- **Recommendation**: reject
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:9853:- **Impact**: medium
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:9855:## Sources Consulted
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:9890:## Assessment
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:9895:## Reflection
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:9899:## Recommended Next Focus

/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:47:## 5. Instructions
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:74:## 6. Research Questions
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:82:7. Compared with CocoIndex's semantic code search, what is unique about Mnemosyne's hybrid memory retrieval story, and where should those systems stay separate rather than merged?
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:87:## 7. Do's
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:97:## 8. Don'ts
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:107:## 9. Examples
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:109:### Example A: Hybrid-search contract finding
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:114:- What it does: The README states that Mnemosyne combines SQLite FTS5/BM25 and sqlite-vec cosine similarity with Reciprocal Rank Fusion, while the wrapper simply forwards quoted search queries to `mnemosyne search`.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:115:- Why it matters: Public should not copy README marketing language into implementation plans without deciding whether the value lies in ranking math, wrapper ergonomics, or agent-facing tool guidance.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:116:- Recommendation: prototype later
