/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:21852:.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/response/profile-formatters.ts:223:    followUps.push(`Use memory_context with specFolder "${uniqueFolders[0]}" for full context`);
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:21910:.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/001-research-graph-context-systems/002-codesight/plan.md:32:Run the packet to 20 total deep-research iterations against `external/` to translate codesight's zero-dependency AST extraction, framework/ORM detector architecture, MCP tool design, per-tool profile generation, scanner heuristics, and blast-radius patterns into Public-actionable recommendations. Iterations 1-3 dispatched cleanly through `cli-codex gpt-5.4 high`; iterations 4-5 fell back to native Read/Grep after the codex CLI stalled in S sleep state for 20-50 minutes (likely API throttling from concurrent codex traffic). After original-charter convergence, the user requested 5 more iterations (6-10) targeting unexplored modules; all 5 were dispatched in parallel as background processes via cli-codex. The read-only sandbox blocked direct writes to `/tmp`, so the orchestrator extracted the assembled reports from the codex `-o` last-message captures or stdout reasoning traces and reconstructed iteration files verbatim. The packet was later reopened in `completed-continue` mode for runs 11-20; those late iterations executed directly in the active Codex session because self-invoking cli-codex from Codex would be circular. Externalized state in `research/deep-research-{config,state,strategy,dashboard,findings-registry}` survives crash and resume; reducer runs keep registry, dashboard, and machine-owned strategy sections in sync.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:21927:.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/001-research-graph-context-systems/002-codesight/scratch/phase-research-prompt.md:78:12. Which Codesight ideas overlap too heavily with phases `003` and `004`, and which are genuinely unique to this phase because they center on AST detector design, profile generation, and source-driven static context artifacts?
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:21997:.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/001-research-graph-context-systems/002-codesight/memory/06-04-26_16-34__5-iteration-deep-research-session-investigating.md:197:5-iteration deep-research session investigating the Codesight repository (TypeScript/Node.js zero-runtime-dep CLI) for concrete improvements to Code_Environment/Public around AST-based codebase analysis, framework/ORM detector architecture, AI-assistant context file generation, MCP tool design, per-tool config profile patterns, and blast-radius analysis. All 12 key questions answered (12/12 coverage), 26 source-confirmed findings, converged on all_questions_answered. 3 of 5 iterations dispatched
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:22000:.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/001-research-graph-context-systems/002-codesight/memory/06-04-26_16-34__5-iteration-deep-research-session-investigating.md:405:5-iteration deep-research session investigating the Codesight repository (TypeScript/Node.js zero-runtime-dep CLI) for concrete improvements to Code_Environment/Public around AST-based codebase analysis, framework/ORM detector architecture, AI-assistant context file generation, MCP tool design, per-tool config profile patterns, and blast-radius analysis. All 12 key questions answered (12/12 coverage), 26 source-confirmed findings, converged on all_questions_answered. 3 of 5 iterations dispatched via cli-codex gpt-5.4 high per user preference; iterations 4-5 fell back to native Read/Grep tools after codex stalled in S sleep state for 20-50 minutes (likely OpenAI API throttling from concurrent codex traffic in another window). All findings remain source-confirmed with exact line citations under external/src/, external/eval/, and external/tests/. Key recommendations: ADOPT NOW the single-canonical-ScanResult orchestration shape, AST-first/regex-fallback/confidence-label discipline, per-tool profile overlay split, F1 fixture harness pattern; PROTOTYPE LATER the zero-dep AST loader, Drizzle schema extraction (with index gap fix), local-only regex import graph; REJECT the 11.2x token savings claim (unreproducible from fixtures) and the blast-radius BFS in current form (depth-cap off-by-one bug).
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:22051:.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/001-research-graph-context-systems/002-codesight/research/iterations/iteration-004.md:30:- What it does: `generateProfileConfig(result, root, profile)` first builds a compact ~1-2k token summary (stack, monorepo workspaces, route/model/env counts, top API areas, top 5 hot files, required env vars), then `switch (profile)` writes meaningfully different content per target:
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:22053:.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/001-research-graph-context-systems/002-codesight/research/iterations/iteration-004.md:37:- Why it matters: This is the most directly transferable pattern in Codesight. `Code_Environment/Public` could borrow the "shared summary + per-tool instruction overlay" split for its `CLAUDE.md` / `.codex` / `.cursor` / `.gemini` directories, but the cargo-cult risk is real: Codesight's current implementation hardcodes example MCP tool names like `codesight_get_blast_radius` into the Claude profile, which would only make sense after a similar MCP surface is built locally.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:22055:.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/001-research-graph-context-systems/002-codesight/research/iterations/iteration-004.md:102:- Iteration 5 should also answer Q12 (cross-phase overlap with 003 and 004): synthesize what is uniquely Codesight (AST detector design + per-tool profile generation + static artifact emission) and what overlaps dangerously (MCP query interface with 003-contextador, dependency graph with 004-graphify).
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:22058:.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/001-research-graph-context-systems/002-codesight/research/iterations/iteration-020.md:31:- What it does: watch mode implies more incrementalism than it has, MCP query tools write `.codesight/` on disk, profile generation mutates root assistant files, and token messaging regains false precision outside `CODESIGHT.md`.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:22062:.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/001-research-graph-context-systems/002-codesight/research/iterations/iteration-005.md:6:The "static `.codesight/` files vs query-time MCP tools" split is not really a split: both layers consume the same `ScanResult` data structure, and the MCP server actually re-runs `writeOutput()` to materialize the same static files on cold cache misses, so the static artifacts are a side effect of running the MCP scan, not a separate pipeline. The architectural value of the static layer is that it lets assistants without an MCP connection still read pre-generated context (great for git-committed `CLAUDE.md`/`AGENTS.md`), while the MCP layer adds filtering and on-demand queries on top of the same data. Iteration 2's "no Drizzle index extraction" finding is reconfirmed: `extract-schema.ts` only handles `primaryKey/notNull/unique/default/references` as field flags, with zero matches for `index/Index/uniqueIndex/composite index` patterns. Cross-phase boundaries are now clean: 002-codesight uniquely owns AST detector design, per-tool profile generation, and static artifact emission; 003-contextador owns the self-healing query MCP; 004-graphify owns NetworkX/Leiden graph math.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:22063:.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/001-research-graph-context-systems/002-codesight/research/iterations/iteration-005.md:48:- What it does: Both `scan()` and `getScanResult()` run `detectProject` + `collectFiles` + the 7 parallel detectors + `enrichRouteContracts`. The differences are: `scan()` respects `userConfig.disableDetectors`, runs plugin detectors and post-processors, prints CLI progress, calls `writeOutput` twice (placeholder then accurate token stats); `getScanResult()` skips the plugin path, uses fixed maxDepth=10, calls `writeOutput` once, caches the result. There is no shared helper.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:22064:.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/001-research-graph-context-systems/002-codesight/research/iterations/iteration-005.md:49:- Why it matters: For `Code_Environment/Public`, the lesson is to factor any analogous "scan + project" pipeline into a single library function that both the CLI and any future MCP/server entry points call, so that toggles like disabled detectors, plugin hooks, and progress reporting are not silently divergent.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:22066:.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/001-research-graph-context-systems/002-codesight/research/iterations/iteration-005.md:80:| Per-tool AI assistant profile generation (CLAUDE/Cursor/Codex/Copilot/Windsurf) | 002-codesight | `generateProfileConfig()` is unique to this phase | adopt the "shared summary + per-tool overlay" split, write into namespaced sections only |
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:22067:.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/001-research-graph-context-systems/002-codesight/research/iterations/iteration-005.md:94:- Q12: answered - The boundary table above shows AST detector pipeline + per-tool profile generation + static `.codesight/` emission belong uniquely to 002, while query-time MCP belongs to 003-contextador and graph math belongs to 004-graphify; 005-claudest does not overlap.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:22068:.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/001-research-graph-context-systems/002-codesight/research/iterations/iteration-005.md:101:This iteration finalized the cross-phase boundary by anchoring AST detector design, per-tool profile generation, and static artifact emission as uniquely Codesight (phase 002), while explicitly assigning the MCP query interface to 003-contextador and graph math to 004-graphify. It avoided 003/004 territory by reading only Codesight's own source files and by defining the boundary as a "do not duplicate" rule for the synthesis phase.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:22077:.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/001-research-graph-context-systems/002-codesight/research/iterations/iteration-001.md:21:- What it does: `scan()` performs the core pipeline once: `detectProject()`, `collectFiles()`, parallel detector fan-out, plugin detector merge, `enrichRouteContracts()`, `writeOutput()`, token-stat calculation, then a second `writeOutput()` with accurate token counts. `main()` loads config, runs that scan once, then layers optional modes on top of the same `result` object for telemetry, JSON, AI config generation, HTML, benchmark output, blast radius, profile generation, and watch mode.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:22079:.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/001-research-graph-context-systems/002-codesight/research/iterations/iteration-001.md:48:- What it does: `generateAIConfigs()` builds one generic narrative summary and writes or appends it into assistant-specific files such as `CLAUDE.md`, `.cursorrules`, `.github/copilot-instructions.md`, `codex.md`, and `AGENTS.md`. `generateProfileConfig()` then creates tighter tool-specific variants, with the Claude profile explicitly instructing the assistant to read `.codesight/CODESIGHT.md` first and use narrow MCP tools for routes, schema, and blast radius.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:22098:.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/001-research-graph-context-systems/002-codesight/research/iterations/iteration-025.md:19:What it does: CodeSight's plugin contract is real in types but lightly proven in use, and its own conclusion is that untested plugin hooks are not a credible extension story.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:22113:.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/001-research-graph-context-systems/002-codesight/research/iterations/iteration-017.md:33:- What it does: profile mode rewrites `.cursorrules`, `codex.md`, `.github/copilot-instructions.md`, and `.windsurfrules` directly. It also writes `AGENTS.md` at repo root when absent in default generation mode.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:22115:.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/001-research-graph-context-systems/002-codesight/research/iterations/iteration-017.md:42:- What it does: the Claude profile literally instructs the assistant to call `codesight_get_summary`, `codesight_get_routes`, `codesight_get_blast_radius`, and `codesight_get_schema`.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:22116:.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/001-research-graph-context-systems/002-codesight/research/iterations/iteration-017.md:43:- Why it matters for Code_Environment/Public: Public should adopt the profile-overlay pattern, not these literal tool names. Hardcoding non-existent tools into a generated profile creates broken assistant guidance.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:22117:.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/001-research-graph-context-systems/002-codesight/research/iterations/iteration-017.md:51:- What it does: the Claude profile says "This saves ~N tokens per conversation" using the unrounded `result.tokenStats.saved.toLocaleString()`.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:22129:.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/001-research-graph-context-systems/002-codesight/research/iterations/iteration-008.md:48:- What it does: `formatter.ts:290` writes "this file is ~X tokens. Without it, AI exploration would cost ~Y tokens. **Saves ~Z tokens per conversation.**" in `CODESIGHT.md`, with all three values passed through `roundTo100()` so the displayed numbers always end in `00` to signal estimation. But `ai-config.ts:213` writes "This saves ~${result.tokenStats.saved.toLocaleString()} tokens per conversation." into the Claude profile file using the raw, unrounded number — which can show values like `12,847` and read like a real measurement.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:22130:.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/001-research-graph-context-systems/002-codesight/research/iterations/iteration-008.md:49:- Why it matters for Code_Environment/Public: This is a small but meaningful UX inconsistency: the same upstream value is rounded for presentation in one surface and quoted unrounded in another, despite both being downstream consumers of the same heuristic formula. If we adopt the per-tool profile pattern (iter 4 finding 2), we should normalize the rounding rule across all surfaces — either always round-to-100 (signaling estimate) or always show raw (only honest if the formula is honest, which it is not). Pick rounding.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:22140:.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/001-research-graph-context-systems/002-codesight/research/iterations/iteration-009.md:30:- What it does: The CLI parser accepts `-o/--output`, `-d/--depth`, `--init`, `--watch`, `--hook`, `--html`, `--open`, `--mcp`, `--json`, `--benchmark`, `--profile <tool>`, `--blast <file>`, `--telemetry`, `--eval`, `-v/--version`, `-h/--help`, plus a positional directory argument. After parsing, `loadConfig(root)` is merged with CLI overrides only for `maxDepth`, `outputDir`, and `profile`; the rest stay as runtime booleans/strings outside the config object.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:22141:.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/001-research-graph-context-systems/002-codesight/research/iterations/iteration-009.md:58:- Why it matters for Code_Environment/Public: This is a clean "skip work, don't filter results" pattern that's worth borrowing wholesale. It pairs naturally with the future possibility of replacing a built-in detector with a plugin (disable the built-in, enable the plugin), and avoids paying the runtime cost of detection just to throw it away.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:22142:.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/001-research-graph-context-systems/002-codesight/research/iterations/iteration-009.md:66:- What it does: `CodesightConfig` exposes `plugins?: CodesightPlugin[]`; each plugin has a required `name`, optional async `detector(files, project)`, and optional async `postProcessor(result)`. Plugin detectors run after all built-ins, and can only merge additional `routes`, `schemas`, `components`, and `middleware` into the in-memory result set before contract enrichment/output. Post-processors run after `scan()` returns and can replace the final `ScanResult`. Within the files read, there are **no in-tree plugin implementations or plugin/disableDetectors tests** (`rg` over `external/tests` returned no matches), so third-party extensibility is inferred from the typed config-module surface rather than demonstrated by fixtures/tests.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:22143:.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/001-research-graph-context-systems/002-codesight/research/iterations/iteration-009.md:67:- Why it matters for Code_Environment/Public: Third-party detectors are possible if a user's config module imports or constructs plugin objects, but this is not a discovered package/plugin registry. Post-processors are a real extension point in code, not obviously vestigial, though they are currently unexercised in the visible test surface. For Public, the lesson is: plugin contracts that aren't tested aren't real. If we add plugin hooks, ship at least one in-tree fixture exercising them.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:22178:.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/001-research-graph-context-systems/002-codesight/research/synthesis-v2.md:144:- **What it does:** `scan()` performs the core pipeline once: project detection → file collection → 7 parallel detectors → contract enrichment → `writeOutput` (twice, for placeholder and accurate token stats). `main()` then layers optional modes (JSON, HTML, benchmark, profile, blast-radius, MCP, eval, watch) on top of the same `result` object.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:22179:.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/001-research-graph-context-systems/002-codesight/research/synthesis-v2.md:153:- **What it does:** Both functions run `detectProject` + `collectFiles` + 7 parallel detectors + `enrichRouteContracts`. Differences: `scan()` respects `disableDetectors` config, runs plugins, prints CLI progress, calls `writeOutput` twice. `getScanResult()` uses fixed `maxDepth=10`, skips plugins, calls `writeOutput` once, caches the result. There is no shared helper.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:22180:.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/001-research-graph-context-systems/002-codesight/research/synthesis-v2.md:154:- **Why it matters:** Cautionary tale for `Code_Environment/Public`: factor any analogous pipeline into a single library function so toggles like disabled detectors and plugin hooks are not silently divergent across CLI and MCP surfaces.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:22181:.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/001-research-graph-context-systems/002-codesight/research/synthesis-v2.md:294:- **What it does:** Builds a compact ~1-2k token summary (stack, monorepo workspaces, route/model/env counts, top API areas, top 5 hot files, required env vars), then `switch (profile)`:
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:22182:.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/001-research-graph-context-systems/002-codesight/research/synthesis-v2.md:301:- **Why it matters:** The single most directly transferable pattern in Codesight. Public could borrow the "shared summary + per-tool instruction overlay" split for `CLAUDE.md` / `.codex` / `.cursor` / `.gemini` directories. Cargo-cult risk: the Claude profile hardcodes `codesight_get_*` MCP tool names, which would only make sense after a similar MCP surface exists.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:22192:.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/001-research-graph-context-systems/002-codesight/research/synthesis-v2.md:561:| Per-tool AI assistant profile generation (CLAUDE / Cursor / Codex / Copilot / Windsurf) | **002-codesight** | `generateProfileConfig()` is unique to this phase | adopt the "shared summary + per-tool overlay" split, write into namespaced sections only |
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:22254:.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/001-research-graph-context-systems/002-codesight/research/synthesis-v1.md:144:- **What it does:** `scan()` performs the core pipeline once: project detection → file collection → 7 parallel detectors → contract enrichment → `writeOutput` (twice, for placeholder and accurate token stats). `main()` then layers optional modes (JSON, HTML, benchmark, profile, blast-radius, MCP, eval, watch) on top of the same `result` object.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:22255:.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/001-research-graph-context-systems/002-codesight/research/synthesis-v1.md:153:- **What it does:** Both functions run `detectProject` + `collectFiles` + 7 parallel detectors + `enrichRouteContracts`. Differences: `scan()` respects `disableDetectors` config, runs plugins, prints CLI progress, calls `writeOutput` twice. `getScanResult()` uses fixed `maxDepth=10`, skips plugins, calls `writeOutput` once, caches the result. There is no shared helper.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:22256:.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/001-research-graph-context-systems/002-codesight/research/synthesis-v1.md:154:- **Why it matters:** Cautionary tale for `Code_Environment/Public`: factor any analogous pipeline into a single library function so toggles like disabled detectors and plugin hooks are not silently divergent across CLI and MCP surfaces.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:22257:.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/001-research-graph-context-systems/002-codesight/research/synthesis-v1.md:294:- **What it does:** Builds a compact ~1-2k token summary (stack, monorepo workspaces, route/model/env counts, top API areas, top 5 hot files, required env vars), then `switch (profile)`:
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:22258:.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/001-research-graph-context-systems/002-codesight/research/synthesis-v1.md:301:- **Why it matters:** The single most directly transferable pattern in Codesight. Public could borrow the "shared summary + per-tool instruction overlay" split for `CLAUDE.md` / `.codex` / `.cursor` / `.gemini` directories. Cargo-cult risk: the Claude profile hardcodes `codesight_get_*` MCP tool names, which would only make sense after a similar MCP surface exists.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:22268:.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/001-research-graph-context-systems/002-codesight/research/synthesis-v1.md:561:| Per-tool AI assistant profile generation (CLAUDE / Cursor / Codex / Copilot / Windsurf) | **002-codesight** | `generateProfileConfig()` is unique to this phase | adopt the "shared summary + per-tool overlay" split, write into namespaced sections only |
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:22288:.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/001-research-graph-context-systems/002-codesight/research/findings-registry.json:169:      "text": "What it does: `generateAIConfigs()` builds one generic narrative summary and writes or appends it into assistant-specific files such as `CLAUDE.md`, `.cursorrules`, `.github/copilot-instructions.md`, `codex.md`, and `AGENTS.md`. `generateProfileConfig()` then creates tighter tool-specific variants, with the Claude profile explicitly instructing the assistant to read `.codesight/CODESIGHT.md` first and use narrow MCP tools for routes, schema, and blast radius.",
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:22289:.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/001-research-graph-context-systems/002-codesight/research/findings-registry.json:181:      "text": "What it does: `scan()` performs the core pipeline once: `detectProject()`, `collectFiles()`, parallel detector fan-out, plugin detector merge, `enrichRouteContracts()`, `writeOutput()`, token-stat calculation, then a second `writeOutput()` with accurate token counts. `main()` loads config, runs that scan once, then layers optional modes on top of the same `result` object for telemetry, JSON, AI config generation, HTML, benchmark output, blast radius, profile generation, and watch mode.",
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:22295:.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/001-research-graph-context-systems/002-codesight/research/findings-registry.json:859:      "text": "What it does: `generateProfileConfig(result, root, profile)` first builds a compact ~1-2k token summary (stack, monorepo workspaces, route/model/env counts, top API areas, top 5 hot files, required env vars), then `switch (profile)` writes meaningfully different content per target:",
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:22296:.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/001-research-graph-context-systems/002-codesight/research/findings-registry.json:913:      "text": "Why it matters: This is the most directly transferable pattern in Codesight. `Code_Environment/Public` could borrow the \"shared summary + per-tool instruction overlay\" split for its `CLAUDE.md` / `.codex` / `.cursor` / `.gemini` directories, but the cargo-cult risk is real: Codesight's current implementation hardcodes example MCP tool names like `codesight_get_blast_radius` into the Claude profile, which would only make sense after a similar MCP surface is built locally.",
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:22299:.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/001-research-graph-context-systems/002-codesight/research/findings-registry.json:1093:      "text": "What it does: Both `scan()` and `getScanResult()` run `detectProject` + `collectFiles` + the 7 parallel detectors + `enrichRouteContracts`. The differences are: `scan()` respects `userConfig.disableDetectors`, runs plugin detectors and post-processors, prints CLI progress, calls `writeOutput` twice (placeholder then accurate token stats); `getScanResult()` skips the plugin path, uses fixed maxDepth=10, calls `writeOutput` once, caches the result. There is no shared helper.",
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:22300:.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/001-research-graph-context-systems/002-codesight/research/findings-registry.json:1105:      "text": "Why it matters: For `Code_Environment/Public`, the lesson is to factor any analogous \"scan + project\" pipeline into a single library function that both the CLI and any future MCP/server entry points call, so that toggles like disabled detectors, plugin hooks, and progress reporting are not silently divergent.",
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:22302:.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/001-research-graph-context-systems/002-codesight/research/findings-registry.json:1747:      "text": "What it does: `formatter.ts:290` writes \"this file is ~X tokens. Without it, AI exploration would cost ~Y tokens. **Saves ~Z tokens per conversation.**\" in `CODESIGHT.md`, with all three values passed through `roundTo100()` so the displayed numbers always end in `00` to signal estimation. But `ai-config.ts:213` writes \"This saves ~${result.tokenStats.saved.toLocaleString()} tokens per conversation.\" into the Claude profile file using the raw, unrounded number — which can show values like `12,847` and read like a real measurement.",
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:22303:.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/001-research-graph-context-systems/002-codesight/research/findings-registry.json:1795:      "text": "Why it matters for Code_Environment/Public: This is a small but meaningful UX inconsistency: the same upstream value is rounded for presentation in one surface and quoted unrounded in another, despite both being downstream consumers of the same heuristic formula. If we adopt the per-tool profile pattern (iter 4 finding 2), we should normalize the rounding rule across all surfaces — either always round-to-100 (signaling estimate) or always show raw (only honest if the formula is honest, which it is not). Pick rounding.",
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:22305:.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/001-research-graph-context-systems/002-codesight/research/findings-registry.json:1987:      "text": "What it does: `CodesightConfig` exposes `plugins?: CodesightPlugin[]`; each plugin has a required `name`, optional async `detector(files, project)`, and optional async `postProcessor(result)`. Plugin detectors run after all built-ins, and can only merge additional `routes`, `schemas`, `components`, and `middleware` into the in-memory result set before contract enrichment/output. Post-processors run after `scan()` returns and can replace the final `ScanResult`. Within the files read, there are **no in-tree plugin implementations or plugin/disableDetectors tests** (`rg` over `external/tests` returned no matches), so third-party extensibility is inferred from the typed config-module surface rather than demonstrated by fixtures/tests.",
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:22306:.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/001-research-graph-context-systems/002-codesight/research/findings-registry.json:2011:      "text": "What it does: The CLI parser accepts `-o/--output`, `-d/--depth`, `--init`, `--watch`, `--hook`, `--html`, `--open`, `--mcp`, `--json`, `--benchmark`, `--profile <tool>`, `--blast <file>`, `--telemetry`, `--eval`, `-v/--version`, `-h/--help`, plus a positional directory argument. After parsing, `loadConfig(root)` is merged with CLI overrides only for `maxDepth`, `outputDir`, and `profile`; the rest stay as runtime booleans/strings outside the config object.",
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:22307:.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/001-research-graph-context-systems/002-codesight/research/findings-registry.json:2041:      "text": "Why it matters for Code_Environment/Public: Third-party detectors are possible if a user's config module imports or constructs plugin objects, but this is not a discovered package/plugin registry. Post-processors are a real extension point in code, not obviously vestigial, though they are currently unexercised in the visible test surface. For Public, the lesson is: plugin contracts that aren't tested aren't real. If we add plugin hooks, ship at least one in-tree fixture exercising them.",
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:22308:.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/001-research-graph-context-systems/002-codesight/research/findings-registry.json:2053:      "text": "Why it matters for Code_Environment/Public: This is a clean \"skip work, don't filter results\" pattern that's worth borrowing wholesale. It pairs naturally with the future possibility of replacing a built-in detector with a plugin (disable the built-in, enable the plugin), and avoids paying the runtime cost of detection just to throw it away.",
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:22312:.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/001-research-graph-context-systems/002-codesight/research/findings-registry.json:3481:      "text": "What it does: profile mode rewrites `.cursorrules`, `codex.md`, `.github/copilot-instructions.md`, and `.windsurfrules` directly. It also writes `AGENTS.md` at repo root when absent in default generation mode.",
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:22314:.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/001-research-graph-context-systems/002-codesight/research/findings-registry.json:3487:      "text": "What it does: the Claude profile literally instructs the assistant to call `codesight_get_summary`, `codesight_get_routes`, `codesight_get_blast_radius`, and `codesight_get_schema`.",
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:22316:.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/001-research-graph-context-systems/002-codesight/research/findings-registry.json:3493:      "text": "What it does: the Claude profile says \"This saves ~N tokens per conversation\" using the unrounded `result.tokenStats.saved.toLocaleString()`.",
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:22318:.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/001-research-graph-context-systems/002-codesight/research/findings-registry.json:3511:      "text": "Why it matters for Code_Environment/Public: Public should adopt the profile-overlay pattern, not these literal tool names. Hardcoding non-existent tools into a generated profile creates broken assistant guidance.",
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:22319:.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/001-research-graph-context-systems/002-codesight/research/findings-registry.json:4009:      "text": "What it does: watch mode implies more incrementalism than it has, MCP query tools write `.codesight/` on disk, profile generation mutates root assistant files, and token messaging regains false precision outside `CODESIGHT.md`.",
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:22762:.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/001-research-graph-context-systems/research/research.md:54:Claudest remains the most composition-friendly pattern source for Public, but v2 now has a much sharper reason for saying so. It still does not mean “best portable source code”: the license story remains incomplete in this checkout, the host assumptions remain Claude-plugin-shaped, and the producer side still omits some analytics-grade fields. What it does mean is that Claudest’s strongest ideas already land inside one owner Public has, and the 20-iteration continuation converted those ideas into a packet-ordered implementation lane instead of leaving them as generic “adopt now” advice. The packet now points to one concrete first move inside Public’s continuity/analytics track: a shared FTS capability helper plus forced-degrade tests, followed by a bounded Stop-hook metadata patch, a reader-owned analytics model, an additive cached-summary fast path, an explicit verifier/discoverer workflow split, and finally portable token-observability contracts. Cached summaries can still be lossy and wrong, startup selection can still be heuristic, and dashboard-grade spend views can still overstate certainty when defaults or estimated token classes are not visibly labeled. That mix keeps Claudest as the primary reference for Public’s next implementation wave, but only if Public imports the humility, boundary discipline, and acceptance-gate logic alongside the feature shape. [SOURCE: 005-claudest/research/research.md:17-19] [SOURCE: 005-claudest/research/research.md:625-668] [SOURCE: research/iterations/combo-stress-test-iter-12.md:15-44] [SOURCE: research/iterations/combo-stress-test-iter-12.md:51-80]
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:22845:.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/001-research-graph-context-systems/research/archive/research-v2-pre-codesight-closeout.md:50:Claudest remains the most composition-friendly pattern source for Public, but v2 now has a much sharper reason for saying so. It still does not mean “best portable source code”: the license story remains incomplete in this checkout, the host assumptions remain Claude-plugin-shaped, and the producer side still omits some analytics-grade fields. What it does mean is that Claudest’s strongest ideas already land inside one owner Public has, and the 20-iteration continuation converted those ideas into a packet-ordered implementation lane instead of leaving them as generic “adopt now” advice. The packet now points to one concrete first move inside Public’s continuity/analytics track: a shared FTS capability helper plus forced-degrade tests, followed by a bounded Stop-hook metadata patch, a reader-owned analytics model, an additive cached-summary fast path, an explicit verifier/discoverer workflow split, and finally portable token-observability contracts. Cached summaries can still be lossy and wrong, startup selection can still be heuristic, and dashboard-grade spend views can still overstate certainty when defaults or estimated token classes are not visibly labeled. That mix keeps Claudest as the primary reference for Public’s next implementation wave, but only if Public imports the humility, boundary discipline, and acceptance-gate logic alongside the feature shape. [SOURCE: 005-claudest/research/research.md:17-19] [SOURCE: 005-claudest/research/research.md:625-668] [SOURCE: research/iterations/combo-stress-test-iter-12.md:15-44] [SOURCE: research/iterations/combo-stress-test-iter-12.md:51-80]
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:26008:# Iteration 003: TOOL/API SURFACE
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:26010:## Focus
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:26013:## Findings
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:26014:### Finding 1: Mnemosyne exposes a deliberately tiny plugin surface, not a full memory platform API
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:26017:- **What it does**: the external repo returns one OpenCode plugin object with exactly five tools plus one compaction hook; there is no HTTP surface, no MCP server, and no secondary registration layer. By contrast, Public’s memory server test suite locks `TOOL_DEFINITIONS.length` at 43.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:26018:- **Why it matters for us**: Mnemosyne’s value on the API side is ergonomic minimalism, not capability breadth. It is a thin agent-facing facade over a backend binary, whereas Public already exposes a broad operational surface.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:26019:- **Recommendation**: prototype later
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:26020:- **Impact**: high
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:26022:### Finding 2: The CLI bridge is safely implemented, but operationally under-instrumented
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:26025:- **What it does**: Mnemosyne shells out with `Bun.spawn(["mnemosyne", ...args])`, avoids shell interpolation, captures both stdout and stderr, and returns a friendly missing-binary message. But the wrapper only exposes `init`, `search`, `add`, and `delete`; it has no health, status, schema, or startup-readiness tool.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:26026:- **Why it matters for us**: the argv-array bridge is worth copying if we ever front an external memory binary, but the missing operational tools would be a regression versus Public’s `ccc_status`, `session_bootstrap`, and richer MCP diagnostics.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:26027:- **Recommendation**: adopt now
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:26028:- **Impact**: high
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:26030:### Finding 3: Mnemosyne’s project/global split is ergonomic, but the actual scope key is weak
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:26033:- **What it does**: the wrapper derives project scope from the current directory basename, special-cases `global` to `default`, auto-inits the project collection on load, and lazily inits global on first global write. Public instead exposes explicit `specFolder`, `tenantId`, `userId`, `agentId`, `sharedSpaceId`, and `sessionId` scope parameters.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:26034:- **Why it matters for us**: the two-surface ergonomics are attractive, but basename-derived identity is collision-prone and too coarse for Public’s governed and session-aware memory model.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:26035:- **Recommendation**: prototype later
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:26036:- **Impact**: high
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:26038:### Finding 4: Mnemosyne has no profile separation; Public does
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:26041:- **What it does**: Mnemosyne offers one fixed tool family and one fixed compaction prompt block. Public supports response `profile` values on `memory_context`, intent-to-profile auto-routing, and runtime-level profile separation in `.codex/config.toml` (`context`, `research`, `review`, `write`, etc.).
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:26042:- **Why it matters for us**: Mnemosyne’s simplicity is nice for adoption, but it cannot express Public’s profile-aware recovery and formatting contracts. Adopting it as-is would flatten a capability Public already uses for routing, safety, and UX.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:26043:- **Recommendation**: reject
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:26044:- **Impact**: medium
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:26046:### Finding 5: The compaction hook is the most transferable idea, but Public’s implementation substrate is stronger
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:26049:- **What it does**: Mnemosyne injects a static memory-usage checklist into `experimental.session.compacting`. Public’s compact-code-graph plugin already does compaction injection too, but through a cached bridge transport plan, with dedupe checks and a plugin status tool.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:26050:- **Why it matters for us**: the novel reusable part is not “compaction hook exists” but “memory-tool reminders survive compaction directly in prompt text.” Public can add that guidance inside its existing transport-plan architecture instead of copying Mnemosyne’s thinner standalone pattern.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:26051:- **Recommendation**: adopt now
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:26052:- **Impact**: high
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:26054:### Finding 6: Mnemosyne’s hybrid retrieval and offline-first story are mostly backend contract, not wrapper logic
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:26057:- **What it does**: the wrapper only quotes the query and calls `mnemosyne search`; BM25, sqlite-vec, RRF, ONNX local inference, and the `snowflake-arctic-embed-m-v1.5` choice are README-level backend claims. Public already has hybrid `memory_search`, session-aware routing, and separately positions CocoIndex as semantic code search via CLI plus one MCP tool.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:26058:- **Why it matters for us**: there is no evidence here for a superior retrieval algorithm at the wrapper layer. The actionable delta is surface design and first-run UX, not replacing Public’s retrieval core with Mnemosyne’s unseen backend.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:26059:- **Recommendation**: reject
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:26060:- **Impact**: high
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:26062:## Sources Consulted
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:26075:## Assessment
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:26080:## Reflection

/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/research.md:190837:.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-007.md:279:- **Recommendation**: adopt now / prototype later / reject / NEW FEATURE
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/research.md:190838:.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-007.md:280:- **Impact**: high / medium / low
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/research.md:190841:.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-007.md:10563:- **What it does**: `tool_status()` returns drawer counts plus two embedded instruction payloads: `PALACE_PROTOCOL` and `AAAK_SPEC`. The protocol explicitly tells the agent to call status on wake-up, query before answering, write a diary after each session, and invalidate stale KG facts before adding new ones. This makes the first memory call double as an operational contract.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/research.md:190842:.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-007.md:10564:- **Why it matters for us**: This is the cleanest MemPalace agent-integration idea in the codebase. It reduces dependency on separate prompt docs because the memory surface itself teaches the right behavior. Public already has stronger startup guidance in MCP startup instructions and `session_bootstrap`, but it does not currently make one memory-facing tool return such a tight “how to use memory safely” contract.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/research.md:190843:.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-007.md:10565:- **Recommendation**: prototype later
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/research.md:190844:.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-007.md:10566:- **Impact**: high
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/research.md:190846:.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-007.md:10570:- **What it does**: MemPalace implements hooks twice: shell hooks for Claude/Codex install docs, and a Python `hooks_cli.py` layer that already abstracts `session-start`, `stop`, and `precompact` for multiple harnesses. The stop hook counts user messages, blocks every `SAVE_INTERVAL`, and emits a save instruction. The precompact hook always blocks and tells the agent to save everything before context loss. Optional `MEMPAL_DIR` auto-ingest exists, but the normal path tells the agent to classify and file the session itself.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/research.md:190847:.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-007.md:10571:- **Why it matters for us**: The reusable value is not the exact shell scripts; it is the timing contract. MemPalace proves that passive capture can sit outside one model vendor and work as a harness-level preservation mechanism. The weakness is that once blocked, safety depends on the agent correctly choosing categories and scope with no governed boundaries.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/research.md:190848:.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-007.md:10572:- **Recommendation**: adopt now
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/research.md:190849:.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-007.md:10573:- **Impact**: high
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/research.md:190851:.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-007.md:10577:- **What it does**: The real source-backed multi-agent feature is `mempalace_diary_write/read`, which stores each agent’s journal under `wing_<agent>/room=diary`. There is no source-backed `mempalace_list_agents` tool, no agent registry in the MCP tool table, and no enforcement that an agent reads only its own diary or avoids other wings.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/research.md:190852:.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-007.md:10578:- **Why it matters for us**: This is a useful reminder that per-agent memory can start as simple namespaced journals, but it is not enough for multi-agent safety. MemPalace’s README sells stronger agent specialization than the implementation currently proves. For Public, this means we should treat diary-per-agent as a lightweight UX idea, not as an isolation or trust boundary.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/research.md:190853:.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-007.md:10579:- **Recommendation**: reject
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/research.md:190854:.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-007.md:10580:- **Impact**: high
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/research.md:190856:.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-007.md:10584:- **What it does**: Scoping is mostly `wing` and `room` metadata. Search, L2 recall, and L3 search all just build `where` filters on those fields. Conversation mining defaults the wing from the folder name if none is passed. Project mining routes files into rooms via folder/filename/keyword heuristics, and on re-mine it deletes all existing drawers for a source file before re-inserting fresh chunks.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/research.md:190857:.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-007.md:10585:- **Why it matters for us**: This is good human-facing narrowing, but it is not safe scope governance. There is no tenant/user/agent/shared-space model, no cursor scoping, and no protected boundary between “my project” and “your project” beyond labels. The upside is ergonomic: the wing/room vocabulary is memorable and agent-friendly. The downside is that it is easy to mis-file or over-share unless another layer enforces scope.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/research.md:190858:.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-007.md:10586:- **Recommendation**: prototype later
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/research.md:190859:.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-007.md:10587:- **Impact**: medium
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/research.md:190861:.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-007.md:10591:- **What it does**: Public’s retrieval path normalizes scope across `tenantId`, `userId`, `agentId`, `sessionId`, and `sharedSpaceId`, binds continuation cursors to that scope, supports session dedup and intent-to-profile routing, and emits recovery hints. Startup instructions already advertise `session_bootstrap`, `session_resume`, and structural recovery order. Save authority is explicit in `generate-context.js`, where the CLI target spec folder is authoritative. Compaction transport is also already externalized into the compact-code-graph plugin, which injects transport blocks at message and compaction boundaries.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/research.md:190862:.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-007.md:10592:- **Why it matters for us**: On agent integration and project safety, Public is ahead. MemPalace contributes a cleaner, more memorable behavioral surface, but not a safer one. The transferable parts are the small ones: one explicit memory protocol surface, and harness-level passive save timing. The scoping and multi-agent model should not be imported wholesale.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/research.md:190863:.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-007.md:10593:- **Recommendation**: reject
