Public reconsolidates on save, archives superseded rows instead of overwriting them, records `logical_key`, `version_number`, `valid_to`, and `superseded_by_memory_id`, repairs active projections when logical identity drifts, and keeps an append-only `mutation_ledger` guarded by triggers against update/delete.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/research.md:191884:.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-006.md:11986:- **Why it matters for us**: On memory hygiene specifically, Public is already beyond MemPalace. We should not import MemPalace’s drawer lifecycle wholesale; we should only cherry-pick targeted ideas like offline corruption repair or a canonical topic-key layer.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/research.md:191885:.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-006.md:11987:- **Recommendation**: reject
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/research.md:191886:.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-006.md:11988:- **Impact**: high
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/research.md:191888:.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-002.md:3:- **What it does**: The stop hook blocks every 15 human messages; the precompact hook always blocks. The shell and Python paths both use “block with a reason” so the agent performs the save, optionally followed by auto-ingest. This architecture protects context before loss without needing a richer storage model.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/research.md:191889:.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-002.md:4:- **Why it matters for us**: Public already has compaction transport via the compact-code-graph plugin, but MemPalace has a stronger explicit checkpointing posture. The idea worth stealing is the enforced preservation checkpoint, not the exact bash implementation.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/research.md:191890:.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-002.md:5:- **Recommendation**: adopt now
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/research.md:191891:.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-002.md:6:- **Impact**: high
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/research.md:191893:.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-002.md:10:- **What it does**: Public has a much deeper retrieval stack: hybrid pipeline search, session dedup, intent routing, `memory_context`, `session_resume`, `session_bootstrap`, JSON-primary context capture, and compaction-safe structural transport. MemPalace is simpler, but its status/bootstrap surface is better at teaching the agent when to query, verify, write diaries, and invalidate facts.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/research.md:191894:.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-002.md:11:- **Why it matters for us**: The architecture takeaway is asymmetric. Public should not copy MemPalace’s backend wholesale. It should selectively import raw-verbatim retention ideas, checkpoint hooks, and protocol-forward memory UX.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/research.md:191895:.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-002.md:12:- **Recommendation**: adopt now
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/research.md:191896:.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-002.md:13:- **Impact**: high
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/research.md:191897:.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-002.md:85:What it does **not** currently have is a raw-verbatim-by-default memory posture comparable to MemPalace's drawer model, a first-class wing/hall/room taxonomy for agent memory navigation, a dedicated L0/L1/L2/L3 wake-up stack for context loading, MemPalace-style stop/pre-compact save hooks that actively block to force preservation, or a separate temporal entity-triple knowledge graph with explicit invalidation alongside the main memory store. The comparison should stay anchored to those gaps rather than repeating generic hybrid-RAG analysis already covered elsewhere.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/research.md:191898:.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-002.md:156:- What it does: `mempalace_status` returns palace overview data plus `PALACE_PROTOCOL` and `AAAK_SPEC`, while the wake-up stack separately loads L0/L1 context for cheap startup continuity.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/research.md:191899:.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-002.md:157:- Why it matters: Public already has resume/bootstrap tooling, but it does not currently use one memory-facing status surface to teach concrete retrieval and save behavior this directly.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/research.md:191900:.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-002.md:158:- Recommendation: prototype later
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/research.md:191901:.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-002.md:169:- What it does: the stop hook blocks every N human exchanges and the pre-compact hook always blocks, forcing the agent to preserve key context before loss.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/research.md:191902:.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-002.md:170:- Why it matters: Public has compaction-oriented structural transport, but it does not currently enforce a comparable memory-preservation checkpoint before context collapse.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/research.md:191903:.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-002.md:171:- Recommendation: adopt now or prototype later, depending on runtime surface
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/research.md:191906:.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-002.md:272:- **What it does**: technical description with code evidence
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/research.md:191907:.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-002.md:273:- **Why it matters for us**: concrete relevance
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/research.md:191908:.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-002.md:274:- **Recommendation**: adopt now / prototype later / reject / NEW FEATURE
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/research.md:191909:.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-002.md:275:- **Impact**: high / medium / low
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/research.md:191910:.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-002.md:14821:   176	**What it does:** Stores verbatim session text. Embeds with sentence transformers. Retrieves by cosine similarity.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/research.md:191913:.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-002.md:15736:- **What it does**: The primary store is one Chroma collection, `mempalace_drawers`, under `~/.mempalace/palace`; AAAK compression writes to a second Chroma collection, `mempalace_compressed`; temporal facts live in a separate `knowledge_graph.sqlite3`; MCP writes also append to a separate JSONL WAL in `~/.mempalace/wal/write_log.jsonl`.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/research.md:191914:.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-002.md:15737:- **Why it matters for us**: This is simple and very hackable, but storage concerns are split across multiple persistence layers with no shared transactional boundary. Public already has a more unified persistence story.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/research.md:191915:.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-002.md:15738:- **Recommendation**: prototype later
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/research.md:191916:.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-002.md:15739:- **Impact**: high
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/research.md:191918:.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-002.md:15744:- **What it does**: A drawer is just raw document text plus light metadata such as `wing`, `room`, `source_file`, `chunk_index`, `added_by`, `filed_at`, and sometimes `source_mtime`, `ingest_mode`, or `extract_mode`. IDs are deterministic, but the strategy changes by ingest path: project/convo mining key off `source_file + chunk_index`, while MCP manual filing keys off `wing + room + content[:100]`.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/research.md:191919:.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-002.md:15745:- **Why it matters for us**: This preserves verbatim text well, but it has no built-in parent/child chunk graph, active-version pointer, or append-only lineage model. Public’s `memory_index` plus chunk/lineage tables are materially richer.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/research.md:191920:.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-002.md:15746:- **Recommendation**: prototype later
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/research.md:191921:.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-002.md:15747:- **Impact**: high
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/research.md:191923:.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-002.md:15752:- **What it does**: Project mining skips a file only if `source_file` exists with the same mtime, and on change it deletes all old chunks then reinserts. Conversation mining skips solely on `source_file` existence. MCP exposes a semantic `mempalace_check_duplicate` tool, but `mempalace_add_drawer` does not call it; it only checks for an existing deterministic ID.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/research.md:191924:.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-002.md:15753:- **Why it matters for us**: The storage layer has duplicate defenses, but they are not globally enforced. That is weaker than Public’s save-time validation, dedup, and append-only lineage discipline.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/research.md:191925:.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-002.md:15754:- **Recommendation**: reject
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/research.md:191926:.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-002.md:15755:- **Impact**: high
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/research.md:191928:.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-002.md:15760:- **What it does**: SQLite runs in WAL mode and defines `entities` plus `triples` tables with `valid_from`, `valid_to`, `confidence`, `source_closet`, and `source_file`. Current facts are idempotent; changes are represented by invalidation, not overwrite.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/research.md:191929:.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-002.md:15761:- **Why it matters for us**: This cleanly separates fact memory from verbatim memory. Public already has `causal_edges`, but MemPalace’s explicit temporal fact windows are a different and reusable boundary.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/research.md:191930:.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-002.md:15762:- **Recommendation**: prototype later
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/research.md:191931:.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-002.md:15763:- **Impact**: medium
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/research.md:191933:.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-002.md:15768:- **What it does**: It batch-scans drawer metadata from Chroma, builds nodes from shared `room` names, derives tunnels from room reuse across `wing`s, and decorates them with `hall` and `date` metadata. Nothing is persisted as a standalone graph store.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/research.md:191934:.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-002.md:15769:- **Why it matters for us**: This confirms the taxonomy is mostly a navigation overlay over metadata, not a magical retrieval backend. The transferable idea is naming/protocol ergonomics, not a backend rewrite.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/research.md:191935:.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-002.md:15770:- **Recommendation**: adopt now
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/research.md:191936:.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-002.md:15771:- **Impact**: medium
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/research.md:191938:.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-002.md:15776:- **What it does**: `migrate.py` reads Chroma’s SQLite tables directly across version mismatches, reconstructs drawer documents and metadata, and imports them into a fresh collection. `repair.py` backs up only `chroma.sqlite3`, scans corrupt IDs, then rebuilds the HNSW index with `hnsw:space=cosine`.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/research.md:191939:.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-002.md:15777:- **Why it matters for us**: This is the strongest storage-layer lesson in the repo. Public already rebuilds derived indexes and forces WAL, but the MemPalace posture of “logical DB first, vector index rebuildable” is worth carrying forward anywhere our vector layer can drift or corrupt.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/research.md:191940:.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-002.md:15778:- **Recommendation**: adopt now
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/research.md:191941:.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-002.md:15779:- **Impact**: high
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/research.md:191943:.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-002.md:15784:- **What it does**: Public persists a normalized `memory_index`, FTS5 mirror, chunk parent/child rows, active lineage projection, causal edges, BM25 rebuild, and enforced WAL mode. MemPalace persists a simpler raw drawer collection plus sidecar stores.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/research.md:191944:.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-002.md:15785:- **Why it matters for us**: Replacing Public’s backend with MemPalace would be a regression. The right move is a new raw-verbatim capture/archive lane that feeds or coexists with Public’s richer index.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/research.md:191945:.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-002.md:15786:- **Recommendation**: NEW FEATURE

/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/research.md:191970:.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-002.md:15860:- **Why it matters for us**: This confirms the taxonomy is mostly a navigation overlay over metadata, not a magical retrieval backend. The transferable idea is naming/protocol ergonomics, not a backend rewrite.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/research.md:191971:.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-002.md:15861:- **Recommendation**: adopt now
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/research.md:191972:.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-002.md:15862:- **Impact**: medium
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/research.md:191974:.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-002.md:15867:- **What it does**: `migrate.py` reads Chroma’s SQLite tables directly across version mismatches, reconstructs drawer documents and metadata, and imports them into a fresh collection. `repair.py` backs up only `chroma.sqlite3`, scans corrupt IDs, then rebuilds the HNSW index with `hnsw:space=cosine`.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/research.md:191975:.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-002.md:15868:- **Why it matters for us**: This is the strongest storage-layer lesson in the repo. Public already rebuilds derived indexes and forces WAL, but the MemPalace posture of “logical DB first, vector index rebuildable” is worth carrying forward anywhere our vector layer can drift or corrupt.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/research.md:191976:.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-002.md:15869:- **Recommendation**: adopt now
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/research.md:191977:.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-002.md:15870:- **Impact**: high
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/research.md:191979:.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-002.md:15875:- **What it does**: Public persists a normalized `memory_index`, FTS5 mirror, chunk parent/child rows, active lineage projection, causal edges, BM25 rebuild, and enforced WAL mode. MemPalace persists a simpler raw drawer collection plus sidecar stores.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/research.md:191980:.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-002.md:15876:- **Why it matters for us**: Replacing Public’s backend with MemPalace would be a regression. The right move is a new raw-verbatim capture/archive lane that feeds or coexists with Public’s richer index.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/research.md:191981:.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-002.md:15877:- **Recommendation**: NEW FEATURE
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/research.md:191982:.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-002.md:15878:- **Impact**: high
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/research.md:195431:    39	#   session_id       — unique session identifier
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/research.md:195588:    38	#   session_id — unique session identifier
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/research.md:196813:     3	- **What it does**: MemPalace keeps `entities` and `triples` in SQLite with `valid_from`/`valid_to`, separate from Chroma drawers. That split is real. But `kg_add` does not require source evidence, contradiction detection is not actually wired into KG ops, and `invalidate` updates all matching live triples with no stronger review step.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/research.md:196814:     4	- **Why it matters for us**: This is the one new architecture that Public genuinely lacks. The risk is governance, not mechanics: uncited fact insertion, ambiguous invalidation, silent drift between fact rows and verbatim memory, and agent-written “truth” that outruns its evidence. If we build this, it needs mandatory citations, authority tiers, correction workflows, and separation from ordinary memory writes.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/research.md:196815:     5	- **Recommendation**: NEW FEATURE
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/research.md:196816:     6	- **Impact**: high
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/research.md:196820:    10	- **What it does**: Room assignment is driven by folder-name maps, filename/path matches, and keyword counts; the palace graph is then derived from shared `wing`/`room`/`hall` metadata. The repo’s own correction note says the famous “palace boost” was mostly metadata filtering, and benchmark notes show roomed routing still trailing the stronger hybrid no-rooms baseline.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/research.md:196821:    11	- **Why it matters for us**: If Public adopted this whole taxonomy, we would be buying label governance, reindex churn, room drift, fuzzy routing mistakes, and operator overhead for a capability that mostly repackages filtering we already have via scope, context types, and graph/search tools. The main thing that could go wrong is spending real product complexity on a memorable metaphor that does not improve truthfulness or recovery enough to justify itself.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/research.md:196822:    12	- **Recommendation**: reject
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/research.md:196823:    13	- **Impact**: low
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/research.md:196896:    86	What it does **not** currently have is a raw-verbatim-by-default memory posture comparable to MemPalace's drawer model, a first-class wing/hall/room taxonomy for agent memory navigation, a dedicated L0/L1/L2/L3 wake-up stack for context loading, MemPalace-style stop/pre-compact save hooks that actively block to force preservation, or a separate temporal entity-triple knowledge graph with explicit invalidation alongside the main memory store. The comparison should stay anchored to those gaps rather than repeating generic hybrid-RAG analysis already covered elsewhere.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/research.md:196967:   157	- What it does: `mempalace_status` returns palace overview data plus `PALACE_PROTOCOL` and `AAAK_SPEC`, while the wake-up stack separately loads L0/L1 context for cheap startup continuity.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/research.md:196968:   158	- Why it matters: Public already has resume/bootstrap tooling, but it does not currently use one memory-facing status surface to teach concrete retrieval and save behavior this directly.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/research.md:196969:   159	- Recommendation: prototype later
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/research.md:196980:   170	- What it does: the stop hook blocks every N human exchanges and the pre-compact hook always blocks, forcing the agent to preserve key context before loss.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/research.md:196981:   171	- Why it matters: Public has compaction-oriented structural transport, but it does not currently enforce a comparable memory-preservation checkpoint before context collapse.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/research.md:196982:   172	- Recommendation: adopt now or prototype later, depending on runtime surface
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/research.md:197161:# Iteration 020: FINAL SYNTHESIS
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/research.md:197163:## Focus
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/research.md:197168:## Findings
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/research.md:197169:### Finding 1: Pre-compaction preservation is the clearest adopt-now transfer
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/research.md:197171:- **What it does**: MemPalace blocks on `PreCompact` and periodically blocks on `Stop` to force preservation before context loss. Public already has an `experimental.session.compacting` surface and a JSON-primary save path, but not a comparable structured preservation checkpoint.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/research.md:197172:- **Why it matters for us**: This is the highest-leverage continuity improvement with the smallest architecture change. The safe Public version is one compaction-time checkpoint that writes curated JSON through `generate-context.js`, with dedupe/last-save guards.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/research.md:197173:- **Recommendation**: adopt now
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/research.md:197174:- **Impact**: high
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/research.md:197176:### Finding 2: Status-as-protocol is worth porting as a tiny bootstrap hint layer
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/research.md:197178:- **What it does**: `mempalace_status` returns counts plus `PALACE_PROTOCOL`, telling the agent when to search, verify, diary-write, and invalidate facts. Public already emits recovery/bootstrap hints, so it can add a short memory-behavior hint block without inventing a second protocol surface.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/research.md:197179:- **Why it matters for us**: MemPalace’s strongest UX idea is not the palace metaphor; it is that the memory system teaches the agent how to use it. Public can get that benefit with 3-5 concrete hints, not a new subsystem.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/research.md:197180:- **Recommendation**: adopt now
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/research.md:197181:- **Impact**: medium
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/research.md:197183:### Finding 3: Raw-verbatim upside is real, but only as selective evidence retention
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/research.md:197185:- **What it does**: MemPalace returns verbatim text at retrieval time, but ingest is not truly “store everything”: project files are chunked and re-mined, conversations keep only the first 8 AI lines per turn, and heuristic extraction drops low-confidence segments.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/research.md:197186:- **Why it matters for us**: The reusable lesson is not full transcript hoarding. It is keeping verbatim supporting evidence for high-stakes memories so later retrieval can show the actual quote or rationale, while Public keeps its structured, auditable save contract.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/research.md:197187:- **Recommendation**: prototype later
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/research.md:197188:- **Impact**: high
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/research.md:197190:### Finding 4: L0-L3 wake-up is a presentation pattern, not a storage model to copy
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/research.md:197192:- **What it does**: MemPalace’s wake-up stack is L0 identity text plus an L1 “essential story” built by sorting stored drawers by lightweight importance metadata and grouping by room; L2 and L3 are filtered retrieval and search over the same store.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/research.md:197193:- **Why it matters for us**: Public already has `session_bootstrap`, `memory_context`, hints, profiles, and progressive disclosure. The right transfer is a “wake-up profile” on the existing retrieval stack, not a second memory engine or AAAK-first startup contract.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/research.md:197194:- **Recommendation**: prototype later
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/research.md:197195:- **Impact**: medium
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/research.md:197197:### Finding 5: A separate temporal fact plane is the only true net-new feature track
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/research.md:197199:- **What it does**: MemPalace keeps `entities` and temporal `triples` in SQLite with `valid_from`/`valid_to`, query, timeline, add, and invalidate operations. But writes do not require evidence, invalidation is broad, and contradiction handling is still incomplete.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/research.md:197200:- **Why it matters for us**: Public does not have this architecture today. It is the clearest long-term differentiator, but only if built with mandatory citations, authority tiers, correction review, and strict separation from ordinary memory writes.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/research.md:197201:- **Recommendation**: NEW FEATURE
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/research.md:197202:- **Impact**: high
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/research.md:197204:### Finding 6: AAAK and diary-centric memory should stay out of the core recall path
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/research.md:197206:- **What it does**: The repo’s own correction note says AAAK is lossy and regresses versus raw mode, and diary writes explicitly store raw AAAK even while the code comments admit compressed AAAK hurts embedding quality.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/research.md:197207:- **Why it matters for us**: This is useful as a side-channel for compression experiments or personal journaling, but it is not strong enough to become Public’s primary recall representation or startup authority.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/research.md:197208:- **Recommendation**: reject
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/research.md:197209:- **Impact**: medium
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/research.md:197211:### Finding 7: Full palace taxonomy is still the wrong center of gravity for Public
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/research.md:197213:- **What it does**: Rooms are assigned from folder names, filenames, and keyword counts; the graph is reconstructed from shared metadata; benchmark notes show palace-mode routing still trails the stronger no-rooms hybrid baseline.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/research.md:197214:- **Why it matters for us**: Public would absorb governance cost, routing drift, and reindex churn for a metaphor that mostly re-labels filtering it already gets from scoped retrieval, context typing, and graph/code-search tools.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/research.md:197215:- **Recommendation**: reject
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/research.md:197216:- **Impact**: low
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/research.md:197218:## Sources Consulted
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/research.md:197244:## Assessment
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/research.md:197249:## Reflection
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/research.md:197253:## Recommended Next Focus
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/research.md:197266:# Iteration 020: FINAL SYNTHESIS
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/research.md:197268:## Focus
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/research.md:197273:## Findings
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/research.md:197274:### Finding 1: Pre-compaction preservation is the clearest adopt-now transfer
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/research.md:197276:- **What it does**: MemPalace blocks on `PreCompact` and periodically blocks on `Stop` to force preservation before context loss. Public already has an `experimental.session.compacting` surface and a JSON-primary save path, but not a comparable structured preservation checkpoint.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/research.md:197277:- **Why it matters for us**: This is the highest-leverage continuity improvement with the smallest architecture change. The safe Public version is one compaction-time checkpoint that writes curated JSON through `generate-context.js`, with dedupe/last-save guards.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/research.md:197278:- **Recommendation**: adopt now
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/research.md:197279:- **Impact**: high
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/research.md:197281:### Finding 2: Status-as-protocol is worth porting as a tiny bootstrap hint layer
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/research.md:197283:- **What it does**: `mempalace_status` returns counts plus `PALACE_PROTOCOL`, telling the agent when to search, verify, diary-write, and invalidate facts. Public already emits recovery/bootstrap hints, so it can add a short memory-behavior hint block without inventing a second protocol surface.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/research.md:197284:- **Why it matters for us**: MemPalace’s strongest UX idea is not the palace metaphor; it is that the memory system teaches the agent how to use it. Public can get that benefit with 3-5 concrete hints, not a new subsystem.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/research.md:197285:- **Recommendation**: adopt now
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/research.md:197286:- **Impact**: medium
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/research.md:197288:### Finding 3: Raw-verbatim upside is real, but only as selective evidence retention
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/research.md:197290:- **What it does**: MemPalace returns verbatim text at retrieval time, but ingest is not truly “store everything”: project files are chunked and re-mined, conversations keep only the first 8 AI lines per turn, and heuristic extraction drops low-confidence segments.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/research.md:197291:- **Why it matters for us**: The reusable lesson is not full transcript hoarding. It is keeping verbatim supporting evidence for high-stakes memories so later retrieval can show the actual quote or rationale, while Public keeps its structured, auditable save contract.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/research.md:197292:- **Recommendation**: prototype later
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/research.md:197293:- **Impact**: high
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/research.md:197295:### Finding 4: L0-L3 wake-up is a presentation pattern, not a storage model to copy
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/research.md:197297:- **What it does**: MemPalace’s wake-up stack is L0 identity text plus an L1 “essential story” built by sorting stored drawers by lightweight importance metadata and grouping by room; L2 and L3 are filtered retrieval and search over the same store.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/research.md:197298:- **Why it matters for us**: Public already has `session_bootstrap`, `memory_context`, hints, profiles, and progressive disclosure. The right transfer is a “wake-up profile” on the existing retrieval stack, not a second memory engine or AAAK-first startup contract.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/research.md:197299:- **Recommendation**: prototype later
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/research.md:197300:- **Impact**: medium
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/research.md:197302:### Finding 5: A separate temporal fact plane is the only true net-new feature track
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/research.md:197304:- **What it does**: MemPalace keeps `entities` and temporal `triples` in SQLite with `valid_from`/`valid_to`, query, timeline, add, and invalidate operations. But writes do not require evidence, invalidation is broad, and contradiction handling is still incomplete.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/research.md:197305:- **Why it matters for us**: Public does not have this architecture today. It is the clearest long-term differentiator, but only if built with mandatory citations, authority tiers, correction review, and strict separation from ordinary memory writes.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/research.md:197306:- **Recommendation**: NEW FEATURE
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/research.md:197307:- **Impact**: high
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/research.md:197309:### Finding 6: AAAK and diary-centric memory should stay out of the core recall path
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/research.md:197311:- **What it does**: The repo’s own correction note says AAAK is lossy and regresses versus raw mode, and diary writes explicitly store raw AAAK even while the code comments admit compressed AAAK hurts embedding quality.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/research.md:197312:- **Why it matters for us**: This is useful as a side-channel for compression experiments or personal journaling, but it is not strong enough to become Public’s primary recall representation or startup authority.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/research.md:197313:- **Recommendation**: reject
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/research.md:197314:- **Impact**: medium
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/research.md:197316:### Finding 7: Full palace taxonomy is still the wrong center of gravity for Public
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/research.md:197318:- **What it does**: Rooms are assigned from folder names, filenames, and keyword counts; the graph is reconstructed from shared metadata; benchmark notes show palace-mode routing still trails the stronger no-rooms hybrid baseline.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/research.md:197319:- **Why it matters for us**: Public would absorb governance cost, routing drift, and reindex churn for a metaphor that mostly re-labels filtering it already gets from scoped retrieval, context typing, and graph/code-search tools.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/research.md:197320:- **Recommendation**: reject
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/research.md:197321:- **Impact**: low
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/research.md:197323:## Sources Consulted

# Iteration 020: FINAL SYNTHESIS

## Focus
FINAL SYNTHESIS: Classify EVERY finding as adopt-now / prototype-later / new-feature-needed / reject. Executive summary.

## Findings
For EACH finding:
### Finding N: [Title]
- **Source**: exact file path(s)
- **What it does**: technical description with code evidence
- **Why it matters for us**: concrete relevance
- **Recommendation**: adopt now / prototype later / reject / NEW FEATURE
- **Impact**: high / medium / low

## Sources Consulted
- [file paths examined]

## Assessment
- New information ratio: [0.0-1.0]
- Questions addressed: [list]
- Questions answered: [list]

## Reflection
- What worked: [approach that yielded results]
- What did not work: [approach that failed]

## Recommended Next Focus
[What to investigate next]

===== 003-modus-memory-main:9762-9882 =====
# Iteration 020: FINAL SYNTHESIS

## Focus
FINAL SYNTHESIS: Classify EVERY finding as adopt-now / prototype-later / new-feature-needed / reject. Executive summary.

**Executive summary:** Borrow **Modus’s operator surfaces**, not its weaker retrieval shortcuts. **Adopt now:** graded `memory_review`, doctor-style summary, and a strict one-retrieval-core rule. **New feature needed:** authoritative `memory_due` and a durable proposal inbox. **Prototype later:** connected-doc appendix, weak-result lexical fallback, and content-level contradiction linting. **Reject:** fuzzy result-cache reuse, permissive markdown ingestion, default write-on-read as policy, and unenforced trust-stage metadata.

## Findings

### Finding 1: Explicit graded review is the best immediate transfer
- **Source**: `external/internal/mcp/vault.go:885-897`; `external/internal/vault/facts.go:160-217`; `.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/cognitive/fsrs-scheduler.ts:39-43,65-71,197-215`; `.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/tool-schemas.ts:298-316`; `.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/handlers/checkpoints.ts:648-790`
- **What it does**: Modus exposes `memory_reinforce`, but only as a success-only reinforce action. Public already has the stronger primitive: graded FSRS review (`AGAIN/HARD/GOOD/EASY`) plus computed `nextReviewDate`, yet the exposed adjacent tool is only `memory_validate(wasUseful)`, which adjusts confidence rather than recording a real review event.
- **Why it matters for us**: This closes the clearest control-plane gap without changing retrieval architecture. It also makes any later review queue trustworthy because review history would record hard recalls and lapses, not only “useful / not useful.”
- **Recommendation**: adopt now
- **Impact**: high

### Finding 2: A doctor-style summary should ship beside review
- **Source**: `external/cmd/modus-memory/doctor.go:13-242`; `.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/handlers/memory-crud-health.ts:378-594`; `.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/tool-schemas.ts:236-273`
- **What it does**: Modus’s `doctor` turns vault health into a one-screen operator report. Public already has deeper health and repair machinery—FTS rebuild, orphan cleanup, degraded/healthy state, confirmation-gated repair—but exposes it as a richer technical envelope.
- **Why it matters for us**: This is mostly presentation work, not backend work. It improves operator trust immediately while preserving Public’s stronger repair semantics underneath.
- **Recommendation**: adopt now
- **Impact**: medium

### Finding 3: Keep one retrieval core; Modus’s split fact-search paths are a warning
- **Source**: `external/internal/index/indexer.go:287-340`; `external/internal/vault/facts.go:290-337`; `external/internal/mcp/vault.go:273-317`; `.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/handlers/memory-search.ts:771-809`; `.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/search/pipeline/index.ts:1-32`
- **What it does**: Modus has multiple fact-search contracts for the same memory objects: `Index.SearchFacts`, a separate in-memory `factStore`, and `Vault.SearchFacts` fallback behavior. Public routes `memory_search` through one pipeline contract via `executePipeline`.
- **Why it matters for us**: This is the strongest architectural lesson from Modus’s weaknesses. Public should keep specialized search surfaces as thin wrappers over the canonical pipeline instead of growing parallel ranking rules that will drift.
- **Recommendation**: adopt now
- **Impact**: high

### Finding 4: `memory_due` is real product value, but it needs an authoritative due-state contract first
- **Source**: `external/internal/mcp/vault.go:273-317,856-897`; `external/internal/vault/facts.go:64-157,160-217`; `.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/cognitive/fsrs-scheduler.ts:65-71,177-215`; `.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/handlers/memory-search.ts:519-520`
- **What it does**: Modus visibly ties search recall, reinforce, decay, and archive to one fact state. Public already stores `stability`, `difficulty`, `last_review`, `review_count`, and opt-in access effects, and computes `nextReviewDate`, but does not expose an operator-facing due queue.
- **Why it matters for us**: This is the biggest missing workflow surface in Public. But it should not ship until “due” is defined as either persisted state (`next_review_at`) or a deterministic derivation from existing FSRS fields.
- **Recommendation**: NEW FEATURE
- **Impact**: high

### Finding 5: Durable proposal inboxes are the best non-retrieval idea Modus has
- **Source**: `external/internal/vault/prs.go:10-118`; `external/internal/mcp/vault.go:681-777`; `.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/handlers/save/reconsolidation-bridge.ts:417-452`; `.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/handlers/checkpoints.ts:681-790`
- **What it does**: Modus persists risky knowledge changes as markdown PR artifacts, then merge/reject outcomes reinforce or weaken linked beliefs. Public already produces advisory reconsolidation recommendations and confidence feedback, but the recommendation is transient and not stored as a durable review object.
- **Why it matters for us**: Public’s next gap is operator workflow, not mutation safety. A persistent inbox for reconsolidation, supersession, promotion, and deprecation proposals would let existing confidence and mutation systems work as a real review loop.
- **Recommendation**: NEW FEATURE
- **Impact**: high

### Finding 6: Connected-doc hints are worth prototyping only as an explanation appendix
- **Source**: `external/internal/index/crossref.go:154-214,248-280`; `external/internal/mcp/vault.go:75-101,901-924`
- **What it does**: Modus builds lightweight subject/entity/tag adjacency and appends connected docs that did not make the main ranked list. The weighting is simple and fixed (`subject 3`, `entity 2`, `tag 1`).
- **Why it matters for us**: The transferable part is explanation, not ranking. Public can test a “related memories” appendix without diluting stronger graph-backed or hybrid scoring paths.
- **Recommendation**: prototype later
- **Impact**: medium

### Finding 7: Lexical-only expansion belongs only in weak-result recovery
- **Source**: `external/internal/librarian/search.go:10-52`; `external/internal/mcp/vault.go:28-58,280-299`; `.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/handlers/memory-search.ts:525-529,771-809`
- **What it does**: Modus asks the Librarian for 3-5 keyword variants, searches each, deduplicates, and caps results. It is lightweight, but ungrounded and union-first.
- **Why it matters for us**: Public already has a more complex retrieval pipeline, so copying this as a mainline branch would mostly add noise and latency. The only credible transfer is a tightly bounded fallback when hybrid retrieval is weak.
- **Recommendation**: prototype later
- **Impact**: low

### Finding 8: Content-level contradiction and duplicate-fact linting is still worth a prototype
- **Source**: `external/cmd/modus-memory/doctor.go:42-158`; `.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/handlers/memory-crud-health.ts:455-555`
- **What it does**: Modus’s `doctor` explicitly flags missing `subject`/`predicate`, duplicate `subject+predicate` pairs, and contradictory values for the same pair. Public’s current health surface is stronger on infrastructure integrity than on fact-content linting.
- **Why it matters for us**: This complements Public’s existing infra health checks instead of replacing them. It is one of the few Modus hygiene ideas that adds net-new value.
- **Recommendation**: prototype later
- **Impact**: medium

### Finding 9: Reject Modus’s fuzzy Jaccard result-cache contract
- **Source**: `external/internal/index/cache.go:10-17,41-119`; `.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/handlers/memory-search.ts:502-529,718-755`
- **What it does**: Modus reuses cached result sets on exact hash hits or Jaccard-similar query term sets. Public’s search surface has too many scoring knobs—scope, archived filtering, session/causal boost, rerank, quality thresholds, trace/profile flags—for fuzzy query reuse to be safe.
- **Why it matters for us**: This is the wrong cache contract for Public. Similar text is not enough to guarantee equivalent retrieval intent or scoring context.
- **Recommendation**: reject
- **Impact**: high

### Finding 10: Reject Modus’s permissive markdown parse/write contract
- **Source**: `external/internal/markdown/parser.go:98-159,161-186`; `external/internal/markdown/writer.go:10-52`; `.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/tool-schemas.ts:220-220`
- **What it does**: Modus treats malformed YAML as body, skips parse failures during scans, and writes markdown directly with minimal validation. That is good for a personal vault, but it optimizes for permissiveness over authoritative ingestion.
- **Why it matters for us**: Public’s memory layer is intentionally more governed. Regressing to Modus’s permissive storage contract would weaken memory integrity for little gain.
- **Recommendation**: reject
- **Impact**: high

### Finding 11: Reject default write-on-read as Public’s default policy
- **Source**: `external/internal/mcp/vault.go:311-317`; `external/internal/vault/facts.go:188-217`; `.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/handlers/memory-search.ts:519-520,797-802`
- **What it does**: Modus treats every returned fact as a successful recall and asynchronously reinforces it. Public already supports access effects, but keeps them explicit with `trackAccess=false` by default.
- **Why it matters for us**: Public’s graded FSRS model is stronger than Modus’s success-only reinforce loop. Default write-on-read would blur retrieval and review, inflate successful-recall history, and make future due-state less trustworthy.
- **Recommendation**: reject
- **Impact**: high

### Finding 12: Reject trust-stage metadata unless it becomes real gating
- **Source**: `external/internal/vault/trust.go:11-82`; `external/internal/mcp/vault.go:610-635,681-777`; repo search on `external/internal/**/*.go` shows trust-stage calls only in trust read/write surfaces
- **What it does**: Modus persists trust stage and exposes get/set tools, but current code does not use that state to gate PR or other mutation tools. It is posture metadata, not enforced runtime policy.
- **Why it matters for us**: Public should not copy symbolic governance. If autonomy stages are added, they must actually gate review, proposal, or mutation flows.
- **Recommendation**: reject
- **Impact**: medium

## Sources Consulted
- `external/internal/index/bm25.go`
- `external/internal/index/cache.go`
- `external/internal/index/crossref.go`
- `external/internal/index/facts.go`
- `external/internal/index/indexer.go`
