     1	package index
     2	
     3	import (
     4		"math"
     5		"sort"
     6		"strings"
     7		"unicode"
     8	)
     9	
    10	// BM25 parameters — standard values, tuned for document search.
    11	const (
    12		bm25K1 = 1.2  // term saturation
    13		bm25B  = 0.75 // length normalization
    14	)
    15	
    16	// Field weights — title matches are more valuable than body matches.
    17	// Validated by ByteRover's benchmark: title 3x, path 1.5x achieves 96% LoCoMo.
    18	var fieldWeights = [6]float64{
    19		1.5, // path
    20		1.0, // source
    21		2.0, // subject
    22		3.0, // title
    23		1.5, // tags
    24		1.0, // body
    25	}
    26	
    27	// fieldIndex maps field names to positions.
    28	const (
    29		fieldPath    = 0
    30		fieldSource  = 1
    31		fieldSubject = 2
    32		fieldTitle   = 3
    33		fieldTags    = 4
    34		fieldBody    = 5
    35	)
    36	
    37	// posting records a term occurrence in a specific document field.
    38	type posting struct {
    39		docID int
    40		field int
    41		tf    int // term frequency in this field
    42	}
    43	
    44	// bm25Engine holds the inverted index and document stats for BM25 scoring.
    45	type bm25Engine struct {
    46		postings map[string][]posting // term → postings
    47		docLens  [][6]int             // per-document field lengths (in tokens)
    48		avgLens  [6]float64           // average field lengths across corpus
    49		numDocs  int
    50	}
    51	
    52	// newBM25Engine builds an inverted index from the loaded documents.
    53	func newBM25Engine(docs []document) *bm25Engine {
    54		e := &bm25Engine{
    55			postings: make(map[string][]posting),
    56			docLens:  make([][6]int, len(docs)),
    57			numDocs:  len(docs),
    58		}
    59	
    60		var totalLens [6]int64
    61	
    62		for docID, doc := range docs {
    63			fields := [6]string{
    64				doc.Path,
    65				doc.Source,
    66				doc.Subject,
    67				doc.Title,
    68				doc.Tags,
    69				doc.Body,
    70			}
    71	
    72			for fieldID, text := range fields {
    73				tokens := tokenize(text)
    74				e.docLens[docID][fieldID] = len(tokens)
    75				totalLens[fieldID] += int64(len(tokens))
    76	
    77				// Count term frequencies
    78				tf := make(map[string]int)
    79				for _, t := range tokens {
    80					tf[t]++
    81				}
    82	
    83				for term, count := range tf {
    84					e.postings[term] = append(e.postings[term], posting{
    85						docID: docID,
    86						field: fieldID,
    87						tf:    count,
    88					})
    89				}
    90			}
    91		}
    92	
    93		// Compute average field lengths
    94		for i := 0; i < 6; i++ {
    95			if e.numDocs > 0 {
    96				e.avgLens[i] = float64(totalLens[i]) / float64(e.numDocs)
    97			}
    98		}
    99	
   100		return e
   101	}
   102	
   103	// search scores all documents against the query and returns top results.
   104	func (e *bm25Engine) search(query string, limit int) []scoredDoc {
   105		queryTerms := tokenize(query)
   106		if len(queryTerms) == 0 {
   107			return nil
   108		}
   109	
   110		// Accumulate scores per document
   111		scores := make(map[int]float64)
   112	
   113		for _, term := range queryTerms {
   114			posts, ok := e.postings[term]
   115			if !ok {
   116				// Try prefix match for partial terms
   117				posts = e.prefixMatch(term)
   118				if len(posts) == 0 {
   119					continue
   120				}
   121			}
   122	
   123			// IDF: log((N - df + 0.5) / (df + 0.5) + 1)
   124			df := e.docFreq(term)
   125			idf := math.Log((float64(e.numDocs)-float64(df)+0.5)/(float64(df)+0.5) + 1.0)
   126	
   127			for _, p := range posts {
   128				// BM25 per-field score with field weight
   129				dl := float64(e.docLens[p.docID][p.field])
   130				avgDl := e.avgLens[p.field]
   131				if avgDl == 0 {
   132					avgDl = 1
   133				}
   134	
   135				tf := float64(p.tf)
   136				numerator := tf * (bm25K1 + 1)
   137				denominator := tf + bm25K1*(1-bm25B+bm25B*dl/avgDl)
   138	
   139				score := idf * (numerator / denominator) * fieldWeights[p.field]
   140				scores[p.docID] += score
   141			}
   142		}
   143	
   144		// Sort by score descending
   145		var results []scoredDoc
   146		for docID, score := range scores {
   147			results = append(results, scoredDoc{docID: docID, score: score})
   148		}
   149		sort.Slice(results, func(i, j int) bool {
   150			return results[i].score > results[j].score
   151		})
   152	
   153		if limit > 0 && len(results) > limit {
   154			results = results[:limit]
   155		}
   156		return results
   157	}
   158	
   159	// docFreq returns the number of documents containing a term (across any field).
   160	func (e *bm25Engine) docFreq(term string) int {
   161		posts := e.postings[term]
   162		seen := make(map[int]bool)
   163		for _, p := range posts {
   164			seen[p.docID] = true
   165		}
   166		return len(seen)
   167	}
   168	
   169	// prefixMatch finds postings for terms starting with the given prefix.
   170	// Only used for short queries where exact match fails.
   171	func (e *bm25Engine) prefixMatch(prefix string) []posting {
   172		if len(prefix) < 3 {
   173			return nil
   174		}
   175		var results []posting
   176		for term, posts := range e.postings {
   177			if strings.HasPrefix(term, prefix) {
   178				results = append(results, posts...)
   179			}
   180		}
   181		return results
   182	}
   183	
   184	type scoredDoc struct {
   185		docID int
   186		score float64
   187	}
   188	
   189	// tokenize splits text into lowercase tokens, removing punctuation.
   190	// Uses Porter-like stemming for common suffixes.
   191	func tokenize(text string) []string {
   192		text = strings.ToLower(text)
   193		var tokens []string
   194		var current strings.Builder
   195	
   196		for _, r := range text {
   197			if unicode.IsLetter(r) || unicode.IsDigit(r) {
   198				current.WriteRune(r)
   199			} else {
   200				if current.Len() > 0 {
   201					token := current.String()
   202					if len(token) >= 2 { // skip single-char tokens
   203						tokens = append(tokens, stem(token))
   204					}
   205					current.Reset()
   206				}
   207			}
   208		}
   209		if current.Len() > 0 {
   210			token := current.String()
   211			if len(token) >= 2 {
   212				tokens = append(tokens, stem(token))
   213			}
   214		}
   215	
   216		return tokens
   217	}
   218	
   219	// stem applies minimal suffix stripping. Not a full Porter stemmer —
   220	// just enough to match plurals and common verb forms.

     1	package index
     2	
     3	import (
     4		"fmt"
     5		"sort"
     6		"strings"
     7	)
     8	
     9	// CrossRef is an in-memory cross-index that connects documents sharing subjects,
    10	// entities, or tags. Built at index time alongside BM25. No new storage — just
    11	// adjacency maps derived from existing frontmatter.
    12	//
    13	// This is Option A from the knowledge graph discussion: connected search results
    14	// without a full graph engine. A query for "Gemma 4" returns the fact, the Atlas
    15	// entity, the beliefs, the articles that mention it, and the learnings — one search.
    16	
    17	// DocRef is a lightweight reference to a connected document.
    18	type DocRef struct {
    19		Path    string
    20		Title   string
    21		Kind    string // "fact", "belief", "entity", "article", "learning", "mission", "role"
    22		Subject string
    23		Rank    float64 // connection strength (number of shared terms)
    24	}
    25	
    26	// crossIndex maps normalized subjects/entities to document references.
    27	type crossIndex struct {
    28		bySubject map[string][]DocRef // lowercase subject → connected docs
    29		byTag     map[string][]DocRef // tag → connected docs
    30		byEntity  map[string][]DocRef // entity name → connected docs
    31	}
    32	
    33	func newCrossIndex() *crossIndex {
    34		return &crossIndex{
    35			bySubject: make(map[string][]DocRef),
    36			byTag:     make(map[string][]DocRef),
    37			byEntity:  make(map[string][]DocRef),
    38		}
    39	}
    40	
    41	// build populates the cross-index from all indexed documents.
    42	func (ci *crossIndex) build(docs []document) {
    43		for _, doc := range docs {
    44			kind := inferDocKind(doc.Path, doc.Kind)
    45			ref := DocRef{
    46				Path:    doc.Path,
    47				Title:   doc.Title,
    48				Kind:    kind,
    49				Subject: doc.Subject,
    50			}
    51	
    52			// Index by subject
    53			if doc.Subject != "" {
    54				key := strings.ToLower(strings.TrimSpace(doc.Subject))
    55				ci.bySubject[key] = append(ci.bySubject[key], ref)
    56			}
    57	
    58			// Index by tags
    59			for _, tag := range strings.Fields(doc.Tags) {
    60				tag = strings.ToLower(strings.TrimSpace(tag))
    61				if tag != "" {
    62					ci.byTag[tag] = append(ci.byTag[tag], ref)
    63				}
    64			}
    65	
    66			// Index by entity mentions in title and subject
    67			// Entities are subjects of atlas docs, but also appear in other docs' text.
    68			// For now, cross-reference by exact subject match across all doc types.
    69			if kind == "entity" && doc.Title != "" {
    70				key := strings.ToLower(strings.TrimSpace(doc.Title))
    71				ci.byEntity[key] = append(ci.byEntity[key], ref)
    72			}
    73			if kind == "entity" && doc.Subject != "" {
    74				key := strings.ToLower(strings.TrimSpace(doc.Subject))
    75				ci.byEntity[key] = append(ci.byEntity[key], ref)
    76			}
    77	
    78			// Scan body for references to known entities (built in second pass)
    79		}
    80	
    81		// Second pass: scan all docs for mentions of entity names in title/body.
    82		// This connects articles to entities they discuss.
    83		entityNames := make(map[string]bool, len(ci.byEntity))
    84		for name := range ci.byEntity {
    85			if len(name) >= 3 { // skip tiny entity names
    86				entityNames[name] = true
    87			}
    88		}
    89	
    90		for _, doc := range docs {
    91			kind := inferDocKind(doc.Path, doc.Kind)
    92			if kind == "entity" {
    93				continue // entities already indexed
    94			}
    95	
    96			ref := DocRef{
    97				Path:    doc.Path,
    98				Title:   doc.Title,
    99				Kind:    kind,
   100				Subject: doc.Subject,
   101			}
   102	
   103			// Check if title or subject mentions a known entity
   104			titleLower := strings.ToLower(doc.Title)
   105			subjectLower := strings.ToLower(doc.Subject)
   106	
   107			for entityName := range entityNames {
   108				if strings.Contains(titleLower, entityName) || strings.Contains(subjectLower, entityName) {
   109					ci.byEntity[entityName] = append(ci.byEntity[entityName], ref)
   110				}
   111			}
   112		}
   113	}
   114	
   115	// ForSubject returns all documents connected to a subject (case-insensitive).
   116	func (ci *crossIndex) ForSubject(subject string, limit int) []DocRef {
   117		if limit <= 0 {
   118			limit = 20
   119		}
   120		key := strings.ToLower(strings.TrimSpace(subject))
   121		refs := ci.bySubject[key]
   122		if len(refs) > limit {
   123			refs = refs[:limit]
   124		}
   125		return refs
   126	}
   127	
   128	// ForTag returns all documents sharing a tag.
   129	func (ci *crossIndex) ForTag(tag string, limit int) []DocRef {
   130		if limit <= 0 {
   131			limit = 20
   132		}
   133		key := strings.ToLower(strings.TrimSpace(tag))
   134		refs := ci.byTag[key]
   135		if len(refs) > limit {
   136			refs = refs[:limit]
   137		}
   138		return refs
   139	}
   140	
   141	// ForEntity returns all documents connected to an entity.
   142	func (ci *crossIndex) ForEntity(entity string, limit int) []DocRef {
   143		if limit <= 0 {
   144			limit = 20
   145		}
   146		key := strings.ToLower(strings.TrimSpace(entity))
   147		refs := ci.byEntity[key]
   148		if len(refs) > limit {
   149			refs = refs[:limit]
   150		}
   151		return refs
   152	}
   153	
   154	// Connected returns the full neighborhood of a query — union of subject, tag,
   155	// and entity matches, deduplicated by path, sorted by connection count.
   156	func (ci *crossIndex) Connected(query string, limit int) []DocRef {
   157		if limit <= 0 {
   158			limit = 20
   159		}
   160	
   161		terms := strings.Fields(strings.ToLower(query))
   162		if len(terms) == 0 {
   163			return nil
   164		}
   165	
   166		// Score each document by how many query terms connect to it
   167		scores := make(map[string]*DocRef)  // path → ref
   168		counts := make(map[string]float64)  // path → connection strength
   169	
   170		addRefs := func(refs []DocRef, weight float64) {
   171			for _, r := range refs {
   172				counts[r.Path] += weight
   173				if _, ok := scores[r.Path]; !ok {
   174					copy := r
   175					scores[r.Path] = &copy
   176				}
   177			}
   178		}
   179	
   180		for _, term := range terms {
   181			addRefs(ci.bySubject[term], 3.0) // subject match is strongest
   182			addRefs(ci.byEntity[term], 2.0)  // entity match is strong
   183			addRefs(ci.byTag[term], 1.0)     // tag match is weaker
   184		}
   185	
   186		// Also try the full query as a single key
   187		fullQuery := strings.Join(terms, " ")
   188		addRefs(ci.bySubject[fullQuery], 3.0)
   189		addRefs(ci.byEntity[fullQuery], 2.0)
   190	
   191		// Sort by connection strength
   192		type ranked struct {
   193			ref   *DocRef
   194			score float64
   195		}
   196		var results []ranked
   197		for path, ref := range scores {
   198			ref.Rank = counts[path]
   199			results = append(results, ranked{ref, counts[path]})
   200		}
   201		sort.Slice(results, func(i, j int) bool {
   202			return results[i].score > results[j].score
   203		})
   204	
   205		if len(results) > limit {
   206			results = results[:limit]
   207		}
   208	
   209		out := make([]DocRef, len(results))
   210		for i, r := range results {
   211			out[i] = *r.ref
   212		}
   213		return out
   214	}
   215	
   216	// Stats returns cross-index statistics.
   217	func (ci *crossIndex) Stats() (subjects, tags, entities int) {
   218		return len(ci.bySubject), len(ci.byTag), len(ci.byEntity)
   219	}
   220	
   221	// inferDocKind determines the document type from its vault path and metadata.
   222	func inferDocKind(path, metaKind string) string {
   223		if metaKind != "" {
   224			return metaKind
   225		}
   226		switch {
   227		case strings.HasPrefix(path, "memory/facts/"):
   228			return "fact"
   229		case strings.HasPrefix(path, "atlas/beliefs/"):
   230			return "belief"
   231		case strings.HasPrefix(path, "atlas/entities/") || strings.HasPrefix(path, "atlas/"):
   232			return "entity"
   233		case strings.HasPrefix(path, "brain/learnings/"):
   234			return "learning"
   235		case strings.HasPrefix(path, "brain/"):
   236			return "article"
   237		case strings.HasPrefix(path, "missions/"):
   238			return "mission"
   239		case strings.HasPrefix(path, "roles/"):
   240			return "role"
   241		case strings.HasPrefix(path, "experience/"):
   242			return "experience"
   243		default:
   244			return "document"
   245		}
   246	}
   247	
   248	// FormatConnected returns a human-readable string of connected documents.
   249	func FormatConnected(refs []DocRef) string {
   250		if len(refs) == 0 {
   251			return ""
   252		}
   253	
   254		var sb strings.Builder
   255		sb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf("## Connected (%d)\n\n", len(refs)))
   256	
   257		// Group by kind
   258		byKind := make(map[string][]DocRef)
   259		for _, r := range refs {
   260			byKind[r.Kind] = append(byKind[r.Kind], r)

 12461	- **Source**: [external/internal/store/store.go#L1462](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/001-engram-main/external/internal/store/store.go#L1462), [external/internal/store/store.go#L3201](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/001-engram-main/external/internal/store/store.go#L3201), [memory-search.ts#L492](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/handlers/memory-search.ts#L492), [memory-search.ts#L772](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/handlers/memory-search.ts#L772)
 12462	- **What it does**: Engram checks for slash-shaped queries, performs an exact `topic_key` lookup first, assigns those hits a forced priority, then merges them ahead of normal FTS results. `SuggestTopicKey()` generates stable family/key pairs such as `architecture/...` or `decision/...`.
 12463	- **Why it matters for us**: Public’s `memory_search` is much stronger overall, but in the inspected handler/pipeline surface it still starts from query/concepts/cursor and hybrid routing, not an explicit stable thread key. For recurring decisions and evolving investigations, a deterministic exact-key lane would reduce dependence on fuzzy recall.

 12469	- **What it does**: Engram’s retrieval path is `mem_search` preview -> `mem_timeline` around one observation -> `mem_get_observation` full body. Public already has summary/snippet/cursor progressive disclosure, but its follow-up step is pagination, not chronology around a chosen hit.

 12476	- **What it does**: Engram sanitizes user input into quoted FTS5 terms and orders results by `fts.rank`, with the `topic_key` shortcut as the only strong override. Public’s `memory_search` runs a staged pipeline with hybrid candidate generation, signal fusion, reranking, evidence-gap annotation, and weighted FTS/BM25 alongside vector and graph channels.

 12483	- **What it does**: Engram presents one memory-oriented MCP surface. Public explicitly routes semantic code questions toward CocoIndex, structural dependency/caller questions toward `code_graph_query`, and session/memory recall toward `memory_search` and `memory_context`.
 12484	- **Why it matters for us**: This split is one of Public’s biggest retrieval advantages. Engram’s model is good for persistent memory, but it has no equivalent to our semantic code lane or graph lane, so its single-surface simplicity should not become a design target for code retrieval.

 12487	
 12488	### Finding 5: Public’s retrieval safety boundaries are materially stronger than Engram’s project-scoped filtering
 12489	- **Source**: [external/internal/mcp/mcp.go#L625](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/001-engram-main/external/internal/mcp/mcp.go#L625), [external/internal/store/store.go#L1462](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/001-engram-main/external/internal/store/store.go#L1462), [memory-search.ts#L531](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/handlers/memory-search.ts#L531), [progressive-disclosure.ts#L367](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/search/progressive-disclosure.ts#L367), [memory-triggers.ts#L207](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/handlers/memory-triggers.ts#L207), [memory-triggers.ts#L291](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/handlers/memory-triggers.ts#L291)
 12490	- **What it does**: Engram narrows retrieval with normalized `project` and optional `scope`. Public binds search continuation to a serialized scope key, validates caller-supplied session IDs against server-trusted sessions, and fail-closes trigger matches when scope filtering cannot be enforced.
 12491	- **Why it matters for us**: Once retrieval includes cursors, dedup, and multi-agent continuity, string-level project scoping is not enough. Engram’s ergonomics are fine, but its retrieval boundary model is weaker than Public’s tenant/user/agent/shared-space discipline.
 12492	- **Recommendation**: reject
 12493	- **Impact**: high

 12515	## Reflection
 12516	- What worked: tracing `Search()` and MCP registration first exposed Engram’s real retrieval contract quickly, and reading Public’s `memory_search` pipeline plus routing/graph handlers made the comparison concrete instead of architectural hand-waving.
 12517	- What did not work: CocoIndex was not usable in this session, so I fell back to `rg` plus direct file reads; the filesystem is also read-only here, so I did not update the phase folder or save memory with `generate-context.js`.
 12518	
 12519	## Recommended Next Focus
 12520	Trace Engram’s export/import and sync replay next, then compare that against Public’s session bootstrap/resume and shared-scope retrieval to see whether stable topic keys survive cross-install merges without weakening Public’s stronger scope and provenance model.
 12521

 12527	# Iteration 008: COMPARISON - RETRIEVAL
 12528	
 12529	## Focus
 12530	COMPARISON - RETRIEVAL: Compare search/retrieval against our Spec Kit Memory (`memory_search`, CocoIndex, code-graph).
 12531	
 12532	## Findings
 12533	### Finding 1: Engram has a deterministic `topic_key` retrieval lane that Public does not expose yet
 12534	- **Source**: [external/internal/store/store.go#L1462](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/001-engram-main/external/internal/store/store.go#L1462), [external/internal/store/store.go#L3201](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/001-engram-main/external/internal/store/store.go#L3201), [memory-search.ts#L492](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/handlers/memory-search.ts#L492), [memory-search.ts#L772](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/handlers/memory-search.ts#L772)
 12535	- **What it does**: Engram checks for slash-shaped queries, performs an exact `topic_key` lookup first, assigns those hits a forced priority, then merges them ahead of normal FTS results. `SuggestTopicKey()` generates stable family/key pairs such as `architecture/...` or `decision/...`.
 12536	- **Why it matters for us**: Public’s `memory_search` is much stronger overall, but in the inspected handler/pipeline surface it still starts from query/concepts/cursor and hybrid routing, not an explicit stable thread key. For recurring decisions and evolving investigations, a deterministic exact-key lane would reduce dependence on fuzzy recall.
 12537	- **Recommendation**: adopt now
 12538	- **Impact**: high
 12539	
 12540	### Finding 2: Engram’s progressive disclosure is better at “show me the surrounding session” than Public’s current pagination
 12541	- **Source**: [external/internal/mcp/mcp.go#L415](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/001-engram-main/external/internal/mcp/mcp.go#L415), [external/internal/mcp/mcp.go#L441](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/001-engram-main/external/internal/mcp/mcp.go#L441), [external/docs/ARCHITECTURE.md#L74](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/001-engram-main/external/docs/ARCHITECTURE.md#L74), [progressive-disclosure.ts#L346](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/search/progressive-disclosure.ts#L346), [progressive-disclosure.ts#L432](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/search/progressive-disclosure.ts#L432), [memory-search.ts#L1023](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/handlers/memory-search.ts#L1023)
 12542	- **What it does**: Engram’s retrieval path is `mem_search` preview -> `mem_timeline` around one observation -> `mem_get_observation` full body. Public already has summary/snippet/cursor progressive disclosure, but its follow-up step is pagination, not chronology around a chosen hit.
 12543	- **Why it matters for us**: Public is more token-efficient at scale, but it still lacks a dedicated “local timeline around result X” retrieval affordance. That would pair well with session continuity and make hit-followup more precise than generic continuation pages.
 12544	- **Recommendation**: prototype later
 12545	- **Impact**: medium
 12546	
 12547	### Finding 3: Engram’s core search is a useful lexical safety baseline, but it is not strong enough to replace Public’s hybrid retrieval stack
 12548	- **Source**: [external/internal/store/store.go#L1518](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/001-engram-main/external/internal/store/store.go#L1518), [external/internal/store/store.go#L3382](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/001-engram-main/external/internal/store/store.go#L3382), [pipeline/README.md#L34](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/search/pipeline/README.md#L34), [hybrid-search.ts#L450](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/search/hybrid-search.ts#L450), [hybrid-search.ts#L972](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/search/hybrid-search.ts#L972)
 12549	- **What it does**: Engram sanitizes user input into quoted FTS5 terms and orders results by `fts.rank`, with the `topic_key` shortcut as the only strong override. Public’s `memory_search` runs a staged pipeline with hybrid candidate generation, signal fusion, reranking, evidence-gap annotation, and weighted FTS/BM25 alongside vector and graph channels.
 12550	- **Why it matters for us**: Engram’s search is easier to reason about, but it would be a downgrade for Public’s memory retrieval quality. The right import is its deterministic key shortcut and input-hardening mindset, not its ranking model.
 12551	- **Recommendation**: reject
 12552	- **Impact**: high
 12553	
 12554	### Finding 4: Public’s retrieval architecture is intentionally split across memory, semantic code search, and structural graph search; Engram should not collapse that separation
 12555	- **Source**: [external/internal/mcp/mcp.go#L124](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/001-engram-main/external/internal/mcp/mcp.go#L124), [context-server.ts#L755](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/context-server.ts#L755), [context-server.ts#L790](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/context-server.ts#L790), [memory-context.ts#L1452](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/handlers/memory-context.ts#L1452), [code-graph/query.ts#L326](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/handlers/code-graph/query.ts#L326)
 12556	- **What it does**: Engram presents one memory-oriented MCP surface. Public explicitly routes semantic code questions toward CocoIndex, structural dependency/caller questions toward `code_graph_query`, and session/memory recall toward `memory_search` and `memory_context`.
 12557	- **Why it matters for us**: This split is one of Public’s biggest retrieval advantages. Engram’s model is good for persistent memory, but it has no equivalent to our semantic code lane or graph lane, so its single-surface simplicity should not become a design target for code retrieval.
 12558	- **Recommendation**: reject
 12559	- **Impact**: high
 12560	
 12561	### Finding 5: Public’s retrieval safety boundaries are materially stronger than Engram’s project-scoped filtering
 12562	- **Source**: [external/internal/mcp/mcp.go#L625](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/001-engram-main/external/internal/mcp/mcp.go#L625), [external/internal/store/store.go#L1462](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/001-engram-main/external/internal/store/store.go#L1462), [memory-search.ts#L531](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/handlers/memory-search.ts#L531), [progressive-disclosure.ts#L367](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/search/progressive-disclosure.ts#L367), [memory-triggers.ts#L207](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/handlers/memory-triggers.ts#L207), [memory-triggers.ts#L291](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/handlers/memory-triggers.ts#L291)
 12563	- **What it does**: Engram narrows retrieval with normalized `project` and optional `scope`. Public binds search continuation to a serialized scope key, validates caller-supplied session IDs against server-trusted sessions, and fail-closes trigger matches when scope filtering cannot be enforced.
 12564	- **Why it matters for us**: Once retrieval includes cursors, dedup, and multi-agent continuity, string-level project scoping is not enough. Engram’s ergonomics are fine, but its retrieval boundary model is weaker than Public’s tenant/user/agent/shared-space discipline.
 12565	- **Recommendation**: reject
 12566	- **Impact**: high

135636	## Findings
135637	### Finding 1: Mnemosyne is not a retrieval-engine upgrade for Public
135638	- **Source**: [external/src/index.ts:106](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/external/src/index.ts#L106), [external/README.md:82](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/external/README.md#L82), [memory-search.ts:482](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/handlers/memory-search.ts#L482), [memory-search.ts:809](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/handlers/memory-search.ts#L809); evidence: both
135639	- **What it does**: Mnemosyne’s wrapper forwards search requests to a backend whose BM25/vector/RRF internals are only README-documented, while Public already runs a visible multi-stage hybrid search pipeline.
135640	- **Why it matters for us**: The transferable value is wrapper ergonomics, not ranking replacement, so using Mnemosyne as a reason to re-architect Public retrieval would be a category error.
135641	- **Recommendation**: reject
135642	- **Impact**: high
135643	
135644	### Finding 2: Compaction-time memory action guidance is the strongest adopt-now idea
135645	- **Source**: [external/src/index.ts:210](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/external/src/index.ts#L210), [spec-kit-compact-code-graph.js:396](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/plugins/spec-kit-compact-code-graph.js#L396), [opencode-transport.ts:142](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/context/opencode-transport.ts#L142), [memory-surface.ts:640](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/hooks/memory-surface.ts#L640); evidence: both
135646	- **What it does**: Mnemosyne preserves memory behavior across compaction with an explicit action card, while Public currently preserves continuity/state but not equally direct memory-tool verbs on the OpenCode surface.
135647	- **Why it matters for us**: A transport-owned, deduped memory reminder would improve post-compaction behavior without changing retrieval authority.
135648	- **Recommendation**: adopt now
135649	- **Impact**: high
135650	
135651	### Finding 3: Mnemosyne’s startup/AGENTS discipline should be reused only after translation into Public terms
135652	- **Source**: [external/README.md:64](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/external/README.md#L64), [context-server.ts:755](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/context-server.ts#L755), [session-bootstrap.ts:163](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/handlers/session-bootstrap.ts#L163), [memory-save.ts:1136](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/handlers/memory-save.ts#L1136); evidence: both
135653	- **What it does**: Mnemosyne repeats “recall early, save after decisions, delete contradictions” in AGENTS and compaction, but Public’s save/delete paths are governed and more constrained.
135654	- **Why it matters for us**: The behavioral pattern is good, but the wording must point to `session_bootstrap`, `memory_context`, governed saves, and careful deletions instead of cheap direct CLI mutations.
135655	- **Recommendation**: adopt now
135656	- **Impact**: medium
135657	
135658	### Finding 4: Read-only `project` / `global` recall aliases are worth prototyping
135659	- **Source**: [external/src/index.ts:118](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/external/src/index.ts#L118), [external/src/index.ts:160](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/external/src/index.ts#L160), [memory-search.ts:529](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/handlers/memory-search.ts#L529), [memory-search.ts:818](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/handlers/memory-search.ts#L818); evidence: wrapper-confirmed
135660	- **What it does**: Mnemosyne gives simple project/global tool pairs; Public already has richer runtime scoping via `retrievalLevel` and broader fallback behavior.
135661	- **Why it matters for us**: Thin read aliases could improve ergonomics, but only if they compile down to existing scope logic and do not invent new authority.

135664	
135665	### Finding 5: Basename-derived project identity is too weak for Public’s memory authority
135666	- **Source**: [external/src/index.ts:28](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/external/src/index.ts#L28), [external/src/index.ts:79](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/external/src/index.ts#L79), [external/README.md:55](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/external/README.md#L55), [memory-save.ts:1265](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/handlers/memory-save.ts#L1265); evidence: both
135667	- **What it does**: Mnemosyne normalizes the current directory basename into a collection key and auto-inits that collection on load.
135668	- **Why it matters for us**: That is fragile across renames, sibling worktrees, and same-name repos, and it is materially weaker than Public’s governed scope model.

135671	
135672	### Finding 6: An ergonomic inline memory intake is valid only as a governed facade
135673	- **Source**: [external/src/index.ts:138](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/external/src/index.ts#L138), [generate-context.js:85](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/scripts/dist/memory/generate-context.js#L85), [memory-save.ts:1128](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/handlers/memory-save.ts#L1128), [memory-save.ts:1273](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/handlers/memory-save.ts#L1273); evidence: both
135674	- **What it does**: Mnemosyne lets the agent store concise strings directly, while Public’s durable save path is JSON-primary, file-backed, preflighted, and governed.
135675	- **Why it matters for us**: A `memory_remember`-style helper could be useful, but only if it produces the same governed save artifacts rather than bypassing them.

135679	### Finding 7: A direct `core=true` shortcut should not be added to Public’s main surface
135680	- **Source**: [external/src/index.ts:140](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/external/src/index.ts#L140), [external/README.md:57](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/external/README.md#L57), [vector-index-store.ts:609](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/search/vector-index-store.ts#L609), [memory-crud-update.ts:67](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/handlers/memory-crud-update.ts#L67); evidence: both
135681	- **What it does**: Mnemosyne collapses “always-on” behavior into a simple `core` tag, while Public reserves that behavior for explicit constitutional-tier handling and budgeted cache/query logic.
135682	- **Why it matters for us**: A shortcut would blur `constitutional`, `critical`, and ordinary memories and encourage over-promotion.
135683	- **Recommendation**: reject
135684	- **Impact**: medium
135685	
135686	### Finding 8: Mnemosyne’s blind append and hard-delete lifecycle is the wrong primary mutation model for Public
135687	- **Source**: [external/src/index.ts:147](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/external/src/index.ts#L147), [external/src/index.ts:193](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/external/src/index.ts#L193), [memory-crud-delete.ts:67](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/handlers/memory-crud-delete.ts#L67), [memory-crud-update.ts:138](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/handlers/memory-crud-update.ts#L138); evidence: both
135688	- **What it does**: Mnemosyne exposes append-plus-delete behavior at the wrapper boundary, while Public wraps updates/deletes in transactions, history, ledgering, checkpointing, and hook invalidation.
135689	- **Why it matters for us**: Public should keep its auditable mutation model and treat contradiction cleanup as structured revision, not as plain-text ID deletion.
135690	- **Recommendation**: reject
135691	- **Impact**: high
135692	
135693	### Finding 9: A native OpenCode memory plugin is viable only as a delegation-only facade
135694	- **Source**: [external/package.json:31](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/external/package.json#L31), [external/src/index.ts:38](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/external/src/index.ts#L38), [session-bootstrap.ts:330](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/handlers/session-bootstrap.ts#L330), [opencode-transport.ts:104](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/context/opencode-transport.ts#L104); evidence: both
135695	- **What it does**: Mnemosyne shows that a thin OpenCode wrapper can feel good, and Public already emits OpenCode transport plans from MCP-owned state.
135696	- **Why it matters for us**: If we build a native facade, it must delegate to MCP for save/search/recovery and keep CocoIndex/code-graph as separate retrieval planes, or it will become a second authority layer.
135697	- **Recommendation**: NEW FEATURE
135698	- **Impact**: medium
135699	
135700	### Finding 10: Optional offline backend readiness is worth prototyping, but Mnemosyne’s runtime stack should not become Public’s default
135701	- **Source**: [external/README.md:18](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/external/README.md#L18), [external/src/index.ts:67](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/external/src/index.ts#L67), [context-server.ts:751](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/context-server.ts#L751), [session-bootstrap.ts:194](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/handlers/session-bootstrap.ts#L194); evidence: both
135702	- **What it does**: Mnemosyne assumes a local binary plus a first-run model download and mainly reports failures through stderr or one install hint, while Public already has explicit bootstrap/readiness surfaces.
135703	- **Why it matters for us**: If we ever support optional local memory backends, they need a real warmup/doctor/readiness flow; Public should not switch its primary path to Bun-plus-binary-plus-model-download.
135704	- **Recommendation**: prototype later
135705	- **Impact**: medium

179594	## Findings
179595	### Finding 1: Static markdown reference integrity is the only clear adopt-now slice
179596	- **Source**: [README.md](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/external/README.md#L72), [claims.ts](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/external/src/drift/claims.ts#L48), [drift/index.ts](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/external/src/drift/index.ts#L17), [path.ts](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/external/src/drift/checkers/path.ts#L8), [edges.ts](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/external/src/drift/checkers/edges.ts#L5), [index-sync.ts](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/external/src/drift/checkers/index-sync.ts#L6)
179597	- **What it does**: Mex extracts typed markdown claims from inline code, code blocks, and dependency headings, then runs zero-token checks for missing paths, dead frontmatter edges, and `patterns/INDEX.md` drift. It already distinguishes real missing references from pattern/template placeholders by downgrading some path misses to warnings.
179598	- **Why it matters for us**: This cleanly maps to our real gap. Spec Kit already has semantic retrieval, trigger recall, causal links, and health tooling, but it does not statically verify that markdown references still point to real files or that pattern indexes stay in sync.
179599	- **Recommendation**: adopt now
179600	- **Impact**: high
179601	
179602	### Finding 2: Dual freshness signals are useful, but only as supplemental trust metadata
179603	- **Source**: [staleness.ts](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/external/src/drift/checkers/staleness.ts#L4), [git.ts](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/external/src/git.ts#L12), [shared-payload.ts](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/context/shared-payload.ts#L22), [session-bootstrap.ts](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/handlers/session-bootstrap.ts#L219), [memory-triggers.ts](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/handlers/memory-triggers.ts#L329)
179604	- **What it does**: Mex emits separate stale-file issues from both elapsed days (`30`/`90`) and commits since last change (`50`/`200`). If git history cannot answer, `git.ts` returns `null`, so the checker silently emits nothing.
179605	- **Why it matters for us**: The days-plus-commits idea is strong, but silent `null` is too weak for Spec Kit. Our current surfaces already distinguish `exact`, `estimated`, `defaulted`, `unknown`, and degraded matching; freshness should plug into that trust model instead of pretending “no signal” means “healthy.”
179606	- **Recommendation**: prototype later
179607	- **Impact**: high
179608	
179609	### Finding 3: Mex’s repair brief flow is worth building as a provider-neutral Spec Kit feature
179610	- **Source**: [sync/index.ts](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/external/src/sync/index.ts#L29), [brief-builder.ts](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/external/src/sync/brief-builder.ts#L7)
179611	- **What it does**: `mex sync` groups issues by file, builds one combined brief, includes the current markdown, issue list, nearby filesystem listings for missing paths, and `HEAD~5..HEAD` diffs for referenced paths, then runs an interactive repair loop and re-checks drift after each cycle.
179612	- **Why it matters for us**: This is the best “AI-assisted” idea in Mex. It converts static failures into bounded, auditable repair work instead of re-running broad doc rewrites. That fits Spec Kit’s packet-local remediation style very well.
179613	- **Recommendation**: NEW FEATURE
179614	- **Impact**: high
179615	
179616	### Finding 4: Command, dependency, cross-file, and script-coverage audits are good ideas, but Mex’s implementation is too Node-centric for direct adoption
179617	- **Source**: [command.ts](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/external/src/drift/checkers/command.ts#L5), [dependency.ts](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/external/src/drift/checkers/dependency.ts#L38), [cross-file.ts](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/external/src/drift/checkers/cross-file.ts#L3), [script-coverage.ts](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/external/src/drift/checkers/script-coverage.ts#L26), [manifest.ts](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/external/src/scanner/manifest.ts#L48)
179618	- **What it does**: Mex validates npm/bun/yarn/pnpm scripts and Make targets, checks package dependencies and versions against `package.json`, flags version contradictions across docs, and warns if package scripts never appear in scaffold text. Non-Node manifests are mostly stubbed.
179619	- **Why it matters for us**: We do need broader wrapper/config/doc parity checks, but our truth surfaces span shell commands, MCP tools, Python, TS, generated docs, and spec packets. Mex’s current inventory model is too narrow to trust in our repo.
179620	- **Recommendation**: NEW FEATURE
179621	- **Impact**: medium
179622	
179623	### Finding 5: Scanner-first setup briefs are useful for bootstrapping, but not for durable truth maintenance
179624	- **Source**: [README.md](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/external/README.md#L50), [SETUP.md](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/external/SETUP.md#L13), [scanner/index.ts](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/external/src/scanner/index.ts#L20), [manifest.ts](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/external/src/scanner/manifest.ts#L12)
179625	- **What it does**: `mex init` builds a structured brief from manifest, entry points, folder tree, tooling, and README, then tells the AI to populate scaffold files from that brief rather than from raw browsing.
179626	- **Why it matters for us**: This is good for first-pass scaffold seeding or packet bootstrap because it reduces exploratory noise. It is not good enough to become an authority layer for Spec Kit memory or documentation parity, especially with shallow non-Node support and no richer trust contract.
179627	- **Recommendation**: prototype later
179628	- **Impact**: low
179629	
179630	### Finding 6: Mex’s GROW loop and `pattern add` workflow should not become part of Spec Kit’s default documentation lifecycle
179631	- **Source**: [ROUTER.md](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/external/ROUTER.md#L56), [README.md](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/external/README.md#L178), [pattern/index.ts](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/external/src/pattern/index.ts#L6)
179632	- **What it does**: The contract requires post-task scaffold growth, and `pattern add` immediately creates a task-pattern template plus a placeholder `[description]` row in `patterns/INDEX.md`.
179633	- **Why it matters for us**: In Spec Kit, partially formed docs become durable retrieval material quickly. Mex’s loop optimizes for compounding coverage, but it does so before truth is secured. That is the wrong default for a trust-sensitive memory system.
179634	- **Recommendation**: reject
179635	- **Impact**: medium
179636	
179637	### Finding 7: Mex’s single drift score should not be promoted into Spec Kit’s primary health surface
179638	- **Source**: [README.md](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/external/README.md#L87), [scoring.ts](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/external/src/drift/scoring.ts#L3), [post-save-review.ts](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/scripts/core/post-save-review.ts#L1019), [validate-memory-quality.ts](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/scripts/lib/validate-memory-quality.ts#L45), [shared-payload.ts](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/context/shared-payload.ts#L22)
179639	- **What it does**: Mex starts at `100` and subtracts fixed costs per issue. That makes score the headline surface even though the underlying findings have very different trust implications.
179640	- **Why it matters for us**: Spec Kit already has richer truth states: block-on-write rules, `REJECTED` post-save outcomes, and certainty/provenance fields. A single score would compress those distinctions and make unsafe or unknown states look deceptively acceptable.
179641	- **Recommendation**: reject
179642	- **Impact**: medium

179676	
179677	## Assessment
179678	- New information ratio: 0.46
179679	- Questions addressed: markdown-first scaffold tradeoffs; checker composition and blind spots; freshness thresholds and git failure semantics; claim extraction behavior; targeted sync design; post-task scaffold growth; overlap with current Spec Kit validation and trust surfaces
179680	- Questions answered: adopt only static reference integrity now; prototype freshness only with explicit trust semantics; build repair briefs as a new feature; defer inventory-backed parity until a cross-surface model exists; reject auto-growth defaults and reject score-first health
179681	
179682	## Reflection
179683	- What worked: tracing Mex in execution order, then comparing each mechanism to current Spec Kit trust and health contracts, made the keep/defer/reject boundaries much clearer than the README alone.
179684	- What did not work: the semantic code search path did not produce usable output in this run, so the synthesis stayed grounded in direct source reads. The validator also emitted the expected sandbox warning about temp-file creation, although it still finished `PASSED`.
179685	
179686	## Recommended Next Focus
179687	1. Implement a narrow markdown drift pass for Spec Kit that checks packet-local paths, frontmatter edges, and index/list coherence with severity-first reporting only.
179688	2. Design a freshness prototype that reports `exact` versus `unknown/degraded` authority instead of silently passing when git data is unavailable.
179689	3. Spec a provider-neutral repair-brief tool that converts failed validations into packet-local remediation prompts with file context and recent diffs.
179690	4. Delay broader command/dependency/script parity until there is a shared inventory model across npm, shell, Python, MCP, wrappers, and spec docs.
179691	
179692

179694	hook: Stop Completed

179697	# Iteration 020: FINAL SYNTHESIS
179698	
179699	## Focus
179700	FINAL SYNTHESIS: Classify every major Mex mechanism as `adopt now`, `prototype later`, `NEW FEATURE`, or `reject`.
179701	
179702	Executive summary: Mex is strongest as a thin markdown-integrity layer, not as a replacement for Spec Kit Memory. The clear win is narrow static reference integrity for markdown surfaces. Freshness is worth prototyping only if we preserve Spec Kit’s explicit `exact` / `estimated` / `unknown` / blocked semantics. Targeted repair briefs are worth building as a new feature. Inventory-heavy parity checks need a broader abstraction first. Auto-growth and score-first health should stay out.
179703	
179704	## Findings
179705	### Finding 1: Static markdown reference integrity is the only clear adopt-now slice
179706	- **Source**: [README.md](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/external/README.md#L72), [claims.ts](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/external/src/drift/claims.ts#L48), [drift/index.ts](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/external/src/drift/index.ts#L17), [path.ts](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/external/src/drift/checkers/path.ts#L8), [edges.ts](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/external/src/drift/checkers/edges.ts#L5), [index-sync.ts](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/external/src/drift/checkers/index-sync.ts#L6)
179707	- **What it does**: Mex extracts typed markdown claims from inline code, code blocks, and dependency headings, then runs zero-token checks for missing paths, dead frontmatter edges, and `patterns/INDEX.md` drift. It already distinguishes real missing references from pattern/template placeholders by downgrading some path misses to warnings.
179708	- **Why it matters for us**: This cleanly maps to our real gap. Spec Kit already has semantic retrieval, trigger recall, causal links, and health tooling, but it does not statically verify that markdown references still point to real files or that pattern indexes stay in sync.
179709	- **Recommendation**: adopt now
179710	- **Impact**: high
179711	
179712	### Finding 2: Dual freshness signals are useful, but only as supplemental trust metadata
179713	- **Source**: [staleness.ts](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/external/src/drift/checkers/staleness.ts#L4), [git.ts](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/external/src/git.ts#L12), [shared-payload.ts](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/context/shared-payload.ts#L22), [session-bootstrap.ts](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/handlers/session-bootstrap.ts#L219), [memory-triggers.ts](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/handlers/memory-triggers.ts#L329)
179714	- **What it does**: Mex emits separate stale-file issues from both elapsed days (`30`/`90`) and commits since last change (`50`/`200`). If git history cannot answer, `git.ts` returns `null`, so the checker silently emits nothing.
179715	- **Why it matters for us**: The days-plus-commits idea is strong, but silent `null` is too weak for Spec Kit. Our current surfaces already distinguish `exact`, `estimated`, `defaulted`, `unknown`, and degraded matching; freshness should plug into that trust model instead of pretending “no signal” means “healthy.”
179716	- **Recommendation**: prototype later
179717	- **Impact**: high
179718	
179719	### Finding 3: Mex’s repair brief flow is worth building as a provider-neutral Spec Kit feature
179720	- **Source**: [sync/index.ts](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/external/src/sync/index.ts#L29), [brief-builder.ts](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/external/src/sync/brief-builder.ts#L7)
179721	- **What it does**: `mex sync` groups issues by file, builds one combined brief, includes the current markdown, issue list, nearby filesystem listings for missing paths, and `HEAD~5..HEAD` diffs for referenced paths, then runs an interactive repair loop and re-checks drift after each cycle.
179722	- **Why it matters for us**: This is the best “AI-assisted” idea in Mex. It converts static failures into bounded, auditable repair work instead of re-running broad doc rewrites. That fits Spec Kit’s packet-local remediation style very well.
179723	- **Recommendation**: NEW FEATURE
179724	- **Impact**: high
179725	
179726	### Finding 4: Command, dependency, cross-file, and script-coverage audits are good ideas, but Mex’s implementation is too Node-centric for direct adoption
179727	- **Source**: [command.ts](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/external/src/drift/checkers/command.ts#L5), [dependency.ts](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/external/src/drift/checkers/dependency.ts#L38), [cross-file.ts](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/external/src/drift/checkers/cross-file.ts#L3), [script-coverage.ts](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/external/src/drift/checkers/script-coverage.ts#L26), [manifest.ts](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/external/src/scanner/manifest.ts#L48)
179728	- **What it does**: Mex validates npm/bun/yarn/pnpm scripts and Make targets, checks package dependencies and versions against `package.json`, flags version contradictions across docs, and warns if package scripts never appear in scaffold text. Non-Node manifests are mostly stubbed.
179729	- **Why it matters for us**: We do need broader wrapper/config/doc parity checks, but our truth surfaces span shell commands, MCP tools, Python, TS, generated docs, and spec packets. Mex’s current inventory model is too narrow to trust in our repo.
179730	- **Recommendation**: NEW FEATURE
179731	- **Impact**: medium
179732	
179733	### Finding 5: Scanner-first setup briefs are useful for bootstrapping, but not for durable truth maintenance
179734	- **Source**: [README.md](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/external/README.md#L50), [SETUP.md](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/external/SETUP.md#L13), [scanner/index.ts](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/external/src/scanner/index.ts#L20), [manifest.ts](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/external/src/scanner/manifest.ts#L12)
179735	- **What it does**: `mex init` builds a structured brief from manifest, entry points, folder tree, tooling, and README, then tells the AI to populate scaffold files from that brief rather than from raw browsing.
179736	- **Why it matters for us**: This is good for first-pass scaffold seeding or packet bootstrap because it reduces exploratory noise. It is not good enough to become an authority layer for Spec Kit memory or documentation parity, especially with shallow non-Node support and no richer trust contract.
179737	- **Recommendation**: prototype later
179738	- **Impact**: low
179739	
179740	### Finding 6: Mex’s GROW loop and `pattern add` workflow should not become part of Spec Kit’s default documentation lifecycle
179741	- **Source**: [ROUTER.md](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/external/ROUTER.md#L56), [README.md](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/external/README.md#L178), [pattern/index.ts](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/external/src/pattern/index.ts#L6)
179742	- **What it does**: The contract requires post-task scaffold growth, and `pattern add` immediately creates a task-pattern template plus a placeholder `[description]` row in `patterns/INDEX.md`.
179743	- **Why it matters for us**: In Spec Kit, partially formed docs become durable retrieval material quickly. Mex’s loop optimizes for compounding coverage, but it does so before truth is secured. That is the wrong default for a trust-sensitive memory system.
179744	- **Recommendation**: reject
179745	- **Impact**: medium
179746	
179747	### Finding 7: Mex’s single drift score should not be promoted into Spec Kit’s primary health surface
179748	- **Source**: [README.md](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/external/README.md#L87), [scoring.ts](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/external/src/drift/scoring.ts#L3), [post-save-review.ts](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/scripts/core/post-save-review.ts#L1019), [validate-memory-quality.ts](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/scripts/lib/validate-memory-quality.ts#L45), [shared-payload.ts](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/context/shared-payload.ts#L22)
179749	- **What it does**: Mex starts at `100` and subtracts fixed costs per issue. That makes score the headline surface even though the underlying findings have very different trust implications.
179750	- **Why it matters for us**: Spec Kit already has richer truth states: block-on-write rules, `REJECTED` post-save outcomes, and certainty/provenance fields. A single score would compress those distinctions and make unsafe or unknown states look deceptively acceptable.
179751	- **Recommendation**: reject
179752	- **Impact**: medium

197169	### Finding 1: Pre-compaction preservation is the clearest adopt-now transfer
197170	- **Source**: [hooks/README.md](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/external/hooks/README.md), [mempal_save_hook.sh](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/external/hooks/mempal_save_hook.sh), [mempal_precompact_hook.sh](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/external/hooks/mempal_precompact_hook.sh), [spec-kit-compact-code-graph.js](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/plugins/spec-kit-compact-code-graph.js), [generate-context.js](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/scripts/dist/memory/generate-context.js)
197171	- **What it does**: MemPalace blocks on `PreCompact` and periodically blocks on `Stop` to force preservation before context loss. Public already has an `experimental.session.compacting` surface and a JSON-primary save path, but not a comparable structured preservation checkpoint.
197172	- **Why it matters for us**: This is the highest-leverage continuity improvement with the smallest architecture change. The safe Public version is one compaction-time checkpoint that writes curated JSON through `generate-context.js`, with dedupe/last-save guards.
197173	- **Recommendation**: adopt now
197174	- **Impact**: high

197176	### Finding 2: Status-as-protocol is worth porting as a tiny bootstrap hint layer
197177	- **Source**: [mcp_server.py](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/external/mempalace/mcp_server.py), [context-server.ts](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/context-server.ts)
197178	- **What it does**: `mempalace_status` returns counts plus `PALACE_PROTOCOL`, telling the agent when to search, verify, diary-write, and invalidate facts. Public already emits recovery/bootstrap hints, so it can add a short memory-behavior hint block without inventing a second protocol surface.
197179	- **Why it matters for us**: MemPalace’s strongest UX idea is not the palace metaphor; it is that the memory system teaches the agent how to use it. Public can get that benefit with 3-5 concrete hints, not a new subsystem.
197180	- **Recommendation**: adopt now

197183	### Finding 3: Raw-verbatim upside is real, but only as selective evidence retention
197184	- **Source**: [searcher.py](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/external/mempalace/searcher.py), [miner.py](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/external/mempalace/miner.py), [convo_miner.py](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/external/mempalace/convo_miner.py), [general_extractor.py](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/external/mempalace/general_extractor.py), [generate-context.js](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/scripts/dist/memory/generate-context.js)
197185	- **What it does**: MemPalace returns verbatim text at retrieval time, but ingest is not truly “store everything”: project files are chunked and re-mined, conversations keep only the first 8 AI lines per turn, and heuristic extraction drops low-confidence segments.
197186	- **Why it matters for us**: The reusable lesson is not full transcript hoarding. It is keeping verbatim supporting evidence for high-stakes memories so later retrieval can show the actual quote or rationale, while Public keeps its structured, auditable save contract.
197187	- **Recommendation**: prototype later
197188	- **Impact**: high
197189	
197190	### Finding 4: L0-L3 wake-up is a presentation pattern, not a storage model to copy
197191	- **Source**: [layers.py](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/external/mempalace/layers.py), [context-server.ts](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/context-server.ts), [memory-search.ts](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/handlers/memory-search.ts)
197192	- **What it does**: MemPalace’s wake-up stack is L0 identity text plus an L1 “essential story” built by sorting stored drawers by lightweight importance metadata and grouping by room; L2 and L3 are filtered retrieval and search over the same store.
197193	- **Why it matters for us**: Public already has `session_bootstrap`, `memory_context`, hints, profiles, and progressive disclosure. The right transfer is a “wake-up profile” on the existing retrieval stack, not a second memory engine or AAAK-first startup contract.
197194	- **Recommendation**: prototype later
197195	- **Impact**: medium
197196	
197197	### Finding 5: A separate temporal fact plane is the only true net-new feature track
197198	- **Source**: [knowledge_graph.py](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/external/mempalace/knowledge_graph.py), [mcp_server.py](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/external/mempalace/mcp_server.py), [README.md](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/external/README.md)
197199	- **What it does**: MemPalace keeps `entities` and temporal `triples` in SQLite with `valid_from`/`valid_to`, query, timeline, add, and invalidate operations. But writes do not require evidence, invalidation is broad, and contradiction handling is still incomplete.
197200	- **Why it matters for us**: Public does not have this architecture today. It is the clearest long-term differentiator, but only if built with mandatory citations, authority tiers, correction review, and strict separation from ordinary memory writes.
197201	- **Recommendation**: NEW FEATURE
197202	- **Impact**: high
197203	
197204	### Finding 6: AAAK and diary-centric memory should stay out of the core recall path
197205	- **Source**: [README.md](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/external/README.md), [mcp_server.py](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/external/mempalace/mcp_server.py), [BENCHMARKS.md](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/external/benchmarks/BENCHMARKS.md)
197206	- **What it does**: The repo’s own correction note says AAAK is lossy and regresses versus raw mode, and diary writes explicitly store raw AAAK even while the code comments admit compressed AAAK hurts embedding quality.
197207	- **Why it matters for us**: This is useful as a side-channel for compression experiments or personal journaling, but it is not strong enough to become Public’s primary recall representation or startup authority.
197208	- **Recommendation**: reject
